channel.title: HoopsHype Rumors - Dallas Mavericks
channel.description: Dallas Mavericks trade rumors and free agency buzz from HoopsHype

item.title: Those priorities remain, according to league sources. …
item.description: Those priorities remain, according to league sources. There is a desire for better center play, true lob threats who mimic the skills of Daniel Gafford and Dereck Lively II (with cleaner injury histories). There is a desire for true two-way defensive players, wings with athleticism and energy on the defensive end and a sweet shooting stroke on the offensive side of the ball. And there’s a desire to lock in a long-term secondary playmaker next to Dončić.

item.title: "That became a constant topic of conversation," Kerr …
item.description: The Warriors remember dealing with it even before the Durant days. Kerr injected an ecosystem of off-ball offensive movement that fully unlocked the shooting greatness of Curry and Klay Thompson. When it vaulted them to the top of the NBA mountain, the outcry was swift and loud: Illegal screens! "That became a constant topic of conversation," Kerr said. "It did frustrate us. Especially if teams countered that by flopping. You run into a slightly moving screen and act like you've just been hit by

item.title: I’m 35 years old, and to be able to be a general …
item.description: As far as you personally, what have these last three days been like? You just got hired on Friday. Mike Schmitz: Yeah, it’s been chaotic in a beautiful way. It’s not lost on me what an amazing opportunity this is. I’m 35 years old, and to be able to be a general manager for such a prestigious organization, working in unison with Masai, obviously Patrick Dumont, and the rest of the ownership group believing in me at a really, really important time — I’m very, very thankful for this chance. I’m su

item.title: "We’ve got a guy who makes it easy for us. His name is …
item.description: Jorge Sierra: Masai Ujiri: "We’ve got a guy who makes it easy for us. His name is Cooper Flagg. Wherever you go around the world, that’s the gem you’re looking for. That’s the type of guy you’re looking for."

item.title: 2019: Pelicans trade Anthony Davis, win the No. 1 pick …
item.description: Jorge Sierra: 2019: Pelicans trade Anthony Davis, win the No. 1 pick in the draft. 2025: Mavericks trade for Anthony Davis, win the No. 1 pick in the draft. 2026: Wizards trade for Anthony Davis, win the No. 1 pick in the draft

item.title: "We’ve got a guy who makes it easy for us. His name is …
item.description: Jorge Sierra: Masai Ujiri: "We’ve got a guy who makes it easy for us. His name is Cooper Flagg. Wherever you go around the world, that’s the gem you’re looking for. That’s the type of guy you’re looking for."

item.description: Jorge Sierra: 2019: Pelicans trade Anthony Davis, win the No. 1 pick in the draft. 2025: Mavericks trade for Anthony Davis, win the No. 1 pick in the draft. 2026: Wizards trade for Anthony Davis, win the No. 1 pick in the draft

item.description: NBA: The results are in for picks 5-14 in the NBA Draft Lottery 2026: 5. LA Clippers 6. Brooklyn Nets 7. Sacramento Kings 8. Atlanta Hawks 9. Dallas Mavericks 10. Milwaukee Bucks 11. Golden State Warriors 12. Oklahoma City Thunder 13. Miami Heat 14. Charlotte Hornets

item.title: Former Dallas GM Donnie Nelson is leading the …
item.description: Former Dallas GM Donnie Nelson is leading the consortium behind this move. Luka Doncic is among the investors, while former player Rimantas Kaukenas, who competed in Italy for Siena and Reggio Emilia, is also among the club representatives. What’s interesting in the report is that the club is also presented as interested in a wildcard for the EuroLeague or the EuroCup.

item.title: There is a desire for better center play, true lob …
item.description: There is a desire for better center play, true lob threats who mimic the skills of Daniel Gafford and Dereck Lively II (with cleaner injury histories). There is a desire for true two-way defensive players, wings with athleticism and energy on the defensive end and a sweet shooting stroke on the offensive side of the ball. And there’s a desire to lock in a long-term secondary playmaker next to Dončić. Reaves, who will decline his player option and become an unrestricted free agent after the sea

item.title: Jason Kidd: This one hurts. Jason Collins was a …
item.description: Jason Kidd: This one hurts. Jason Collins was a pioneer. He had courage like you've never seen. He was an incredible teammate. And having him in Brooklyn at the start of my coaching journey meant so much. Those who knew him were blessed to call him a friend. You are already missed my brother. Rest in power. 💔

item.title: 76ers tried to make trades for Donte DiVincenzo, Naji Marshall, Ryan Dunn, Brandin Podziemski at deadline
item.description: Among other players the source said the Sixers made runs at, unsuccessfully: Naji Marshall of the Dallas Mavericks, Donte DiVincenzo of the Minnesota Timberwolves, Brandin Podziemski of the Golden State Warriors and Ryan Dunn of the Phoenix Suns. None of those players were moved.

item.title: “I’ve known him for many, many years,” Ujiri said of …
item.description: Masai Ujiri made his first big move last week, when the Mavericks hired Mike Schmitz as general manager. Schmitz got his start working for the independent scouting service DraftExpress. He was an ESPN analyst for five years and then became the Portland Trail Blazers assistant general manager in 2022. Although Portland’s draft success was mixed in Schmitz’s four years there, he helped drive the decision to trade for Deni Avdija, who was named an All-Star this past season. “I’ve known him for many

item.title: Grant Hill on Co-Winning ROY with Jason Kidd: “I got …
item.description: NBA Courtside: Grant Hill on Co-Winning ROY with Jason Kidd: “I got robbed. J Kidd knows that, that’s my guy we came in together. You know, I always joke about it and I always tell him this too. We were teammates at the Nike camp in high school. I don’t think he passed me the ball one time. He was rising sophomore and I’m a rising senior, he supposed to be this great point guard and he never passed me the ball at Nike camp. And then they beat us in the legendary tournament matchup where Cal beat

item.title: A report stating that Los Angeles Lakers stars Luka …
item.description: A report stating that Los Angeles Lakers stars Luka Doncic and LeBron James don’t get along is not true, sources told Dallas Hoops Journal. Doncic and James have a positive relationship and Doncic wants James to re-sign with the Lakers this summer, sources said. While growing up in Slovenia, Doncic looked up to James and values having him as a teammate, sources said.

item.title: BYU forward AJ Dybantsa met with the Wizards in …
item.description: Marc J. Spears: BYU forward AJ Dybantsa met with the Wizards in Chicago during the NBA pre-draft camp. Washington is supposed to visit with “multiple players” in DC for the top pick, source days. Dybantsa also met with Utah, Chicago, Sacramento, Brooklyn, Atlanta, Dallas, Memphis & the Clippers.

item.title: Dallas Mavericks rookie Cooper Flagg is in a …
item.description: The rumors are true. Dallas Mavericks rookie Cooper Flagg is in a relationship with Duke University center Arianna Roberson. In a TikTok posted to her account on Thursday, May 14, Roberson, 20, and Flagg, 19, hold hands and embrace in a compilation of clips from their tropical vacation in the the Turks and Caicos Islands.

item.title: Team sources told The Athletic that there were never …
item.description: One bright spot for the Wolves is that they appear set to bring the front-office staff back next year intact. Connelly was a candidate for the top job in Dallas, but team sources told The Athletic that there were never any discussions between the Mavericks and Connelly before Masai Ujiri was hired.

item.title: “Basketball used to be a two-footed sport,” Adams …
item.description: The first clue occurred to me several years ago in a conference room at the Warriors practice facility in San Francisco, during a technical discussion about biomechanics and basketball. At the time, Klay Thompson was still rehabbing from a brutal one-two punch of traumatic lower-body injuries that upended his prime and likely shortened the team’s dynasty. When our meeting wrapped up and the room fell into that unique silence that signals that a group is ready to disperse, Ron Adams, the longtime

item.title: Chandler Parsons when he signed with Dallas: I never wanted to leave Houston
item.description: Chandler Parsons: I wanted to go back and then Mark Cuban offered me the three-year deal in Dallas. And they get like three days or whatever to match through contract if you're a restricted free agent versus an unrestricted free agent. And they didn't did it. They didn't match it. So, like it is bittersweet because like I never wanted to leave Houston. I love Houston. I loved playing for Kevin McCale. I loved playing with James and Dwight.

item.title: Mavs part ways with head coach Jason Kidd
item.description: Mavs PR: The Dallas Mavericks announced today that the team has mutually agreed to part ways with head coach Jason Kidd. The organization will immediately begin a comprehensive search for its next head coach. Full release: https:// nba.com/mavs/news/dall as-mavericks-and-jason-kidd-mutually-agree-to-part-ways

item.title: Sources told ESPN that he has four years and more than …
item.description: Tim MacMahon: The Mavericks announce that they have parted ways with coach Jason Kidd. Sources told ESPN that he has four years and more than $40 million remaining on his contract, but Patrick Dumont authorized Masai Ujiri to make this decision.

item.title: “Jason has had a meaningful impact on the Dallas …
item.description: Mike Curtis: Masai Ujiri on parting ways with Jason Kidd: “Jason has had a meaningful impact on the Dallas Mavericks, both as a Hall of Fame player and as the head coach who helped lead this franchise back to the NBA Finals. We are thankful for Jason’s leadership, his professionalism and his commitment to the team. In my short time here, I’ve developed an enormous amount of respect for what he has built. He will always be an important part of the Mavericks family.”

item.title: Kidd was kept out of the loop in the process that …
item.description: Sources said that Kidd had expressed a desire to be promoted to president of basketball operations after general manager Nico Harrison was fired in November, but Dumont informed Kidd months ago that he wouldn't be considered for the front office. Kidd was kept out of the loop in the process that resulted in Ujiri's hiring, sources said.

item.title: Kidd's frustration with Doncic's frequent conditioning …
item.description: Kidd has insisted that he wasn't aware of the deal until "the 11th hour," doubling down on that explanation in early April after minority owner Mark Cuban appeared on a podcast and accused Kidd of being directly involved in the decision to trade Doncic. Kidd's frustration with Doncic's frequent conditioning issues was well known throughout the organization.

item.title: Will Hardy now has the seventh-longest active tenure …
item.description: Tim Reynolds: Want to hear something wild? Will Hardy now has the seventh-longest active tenure among all NBA coaches, after Jason Kidd's ouster in Dallas. Erik Spoelstra, Steve Kerr, Tyronn Lue, Mark Daigneault, Chris Finch, Rick Carlisle ... and then Will Hardy.

item.title: 11 coaches have made the NBA Finals since 2020
item.description: Tim Reynolds: 11 coaches have made the NBA Finals since 2020. 6 are no longer with the teams they took to those Finals. 2024: Jason Kidd, Dallas 2023: Michael Malone, Denver 2022: Ime Udoka, Boston 2021: Monty Williams, Phoenix 2021: Mike Budenholzer, Milwaukee 2020: Frank Vogel, Lakers

item.title: Matt Riccardi will not return with the Mavs
item.description: Jake Fischer: I’m told Mavericks executive Matt Riccardi is also departing the organization. Riccardi, who served as Dallas’ co-general manager this season in the wake of Nico Harrison’s exit, was the runner-up to Masai Ujiri in Patrick Dumont’s process to find a new president of basketball operations.

item.title: League sources tell The Stein Line that Kidd and Ujiri …
item.description: League sources tell The Stein Line that Kidd and Ujiri had multiple conversations over the past two weeks — including a lengthy meeting that included new general manager Mike Schmitz before Ujiri and Schmitz traveled to Chicago for last week's NBA Draft combine — but rumbles nonetheless swirled in the Windy City throughout the combine that a coaching change in Dallas could indeed be imminent.

item.title: The top candidates to replace Kidd were not …
item.description: The top candidates to replace Kidd were not immediately apparent Tuesday night, but San Antonio's Sean Sweeney and Minnesota's Micah Nori — widely regarded around the league as future head coaches — are both known quantities in Dallas.

item.title: Dumont has committed to what industry sources say is …
item.description: Dumont has committed to what industry sources say is comfortably an eight-figure annual salary to land Ujiri and blessed the decision to let Kidd go with so many years and dollars still left on the coach's contract.

item.title: Current minority owner of the Mavericks, Mark Cuban, …
item.description: However, the organization is now moving in a new direction under president Masai Ujiri. "As we evaluate the future of our basketball program, we believe this is the right moment for a new direction for our team," the president said in a statement, as per ESPN. Current minority owner of the Mavericks, Mark Cuban, sent an email to share his thoughts on the news. "Obviously I'm a jkidd fan. So I'm surprised and disappointed," he wrote. "But we have to give masai a chance to see what happens. He has

item.title: Kyrie Irving to draw trade interest?
item.description: Yet there is an anticipation leaguewide that the Mavericks are likely to receive trade interest in Irving this offseason whether they want it or not ... even after the 34-year-old's full season of inactivity.

item.title: Masai Ujiri says Luka Doncic trade played no part in dismissal of Jason Kidd
item.description: Clutch Points: "That trade has played no part in like how I have thought about anything. ... I'm in no position to criticize or blame." Masai Ujiri on whether the Luka Doncic trade played a part in the Mavs moving on from Jason Kidd.

item.title: “I think a new slate was a good way to look at this. I …
item.description: Abby Jones: Masai Ujiri says this on the departure of Dallas Mavericks Head Coach: “I think a new slate was a good way to look at this. I feel sometimes in this organization, we needed clarity in where we're going, rather than sometimes lots of things in the mix. We need to literally work in one direction and how we build this team, how we create winning and that's kind of how I looked at it, just going forward and building winning.”

item.title: He shares more, “I don’t think there’s a timeline …
item.description: Abby Jones: Masai Ujiri says he doesn’t know how long the Head Coach search will take but it will be a comprehensive search. He shares more, “I don’t think there’s a timeline right now. But it’s something that we want to get done the right way.”

item.title: “Every decision we are going to make here is going to …
item.description: Abby Jones: Masai Ujiri states that the Dallas Mavericks are looking at the future of organization, building around Cooper Flagg. “Every decision we are going to make here is going to be future based. We are looking at the future. We have a 19-year-old on this team that can take us somewhere great. We have to think that way.”

item.title: Masai Ujiri says there is a “huge curiosity” to see …
item.description: Christian Clark: Masai Ujiri says there is a “huge curiosity” to see Kyrie play next to Cooper Flagg.

item.title: Multiple contenders monitoring what happens with Kyrie Irving in Dallas
item.description: Pat McAffee: "The only Mark Cuban guy left on the Mavericks is Kyrie Irving.. There's multiple contenders around the NBA that are very much monitoring what happens with Kyrie Irving in Dallas" ~ @ShamsCharania

item.title: Mavs sources believe that Jason Kidd had a level of …
item.description: Pat McAffee: "Masai Ujiri has full authority to do whatever he wants with the direction of this Mavericks team.. Mavs sources believe that Jason Kidd had a level of support for the Luka Doncic trade.. This is a complete restart for the entire organization" ~ @ShamsCharania

item.title: Masai Ujiri on Mavericks head coaching search: First-time, experienced, college, European, every stone will be unturned
item.description: Mike Curtis: Masai Ujiri on how much previous head coaching experience will play a role into the hire of a new coach: “It’s an open search…Out minds are very open. We’re going to look everywhere. Every stone unturned and we will really, really look at what’s best for this organization. First-time, experienced, college, European, everywhere. Whatever is best for this organization going forward.”

item.title: Masai Ujiri says that “nobody has received a phone …
item.description: Abby Jones: Masai Ujiri says that “nobody has received a phone call from him” in search of a new Head Coach, he felt it was disrespectful to Jason Kidd to speak with other candidates at this time.

item.title: “Kevin Durant once told me that there's only one Kyrie …
item.description: Abby Jones: Masai Ujiri speaks on the future of Kyrie Irving in Dallas after the departure of HC, Jason Kidd. “Kevin Durant once told me that there's only one Kyrie Irving walking around in the world. I think we have to figure out a way, how Kyrie fits with our program. I've had those conversations with Kyrie, up till yesterday and I think Kyrie will fit. There's a huge curiosity in our minds to see how Kyrie fits playing with Cooper Flagg … and I think we owe that to this organization.”

item.title: But when I look at the Trail Blazers roster, that …
item.description: Draymond Green: "Jason Kidd from Oakland. Damian Lillard also from Oakland. Point guard. You look at Scoot Henderson, a young guard. How can Jason Kidd help him? So when you take a step back and you look at those jobs, I definitely can see Jason Kidd taking the Trail Blazers job. Now, however, it's been widely known that the owner of their team doesn't want to pay the head coach. It's been widely publicized. A lot of people have been talking about it. And quite honestly, you're making $10 millio

item.title: There are rumors flying around, and I'm not going to …
item.description: Tim McMahon: "I would say a couple things. There are rumors flying around, and I'm not going to say, there's a big-name college coach being rumored. Masai's track record is not necessarily swinging for big names. It's trying to find the next great coach. He likes discoveries."

item.title: Nowitzki also has the record for most regular-season …
item.description: Fun fact: Nowitzki also has the record for most regular-season wins without All-NBA teammates with 804. Dallas fans should be grateful to this day that Nowitzki was loyal enough to never ask out, because a lot of other stars without star help surely would have gotten frustrated enough to demand a trade.

item.title: Cooper Flagg, Dylan Harper, Kon Knueppel headline All-Rookie First Team
item.description: NBA Communications: The 2025-26 Kia NBA All-Rookie First Team: Cedric Coward, @memgrizz VJ Edgecombe, @sixers Cooper Flagg, @dallasmavs Dylan Harper, @spurs Kon Knueppel, @hornets

item.title: Shams: Multiple contenders are keeping an eye on Kyrie Irving right now
item.description: The Dallas Mavericks are remaking their franchise around young star Cooper Flagg, which naturally leads to questions about how Kyrie Irving fits into that mix. "Multiple contenders are keeping an eye on Irving right now," ESPN's Shams Charania reported on Wednesday's NBA Today (2:38 mark). "He's got a guaranteed year left on his deal and then he's got a player option, so that does give him some leverage on his future. He's 34 years old, he's a championship player in this league, but he's got a l

item.title: Jason Kidd pens goodbye to Dallas
item.description: Jason Kidd: Thank you, Dallas.

item.title: NBA exec says Dawn Staley could be candidate for Mavs head coaching job
item.description: Dawn Staley later said that going through that process made her believe that “no NBA team is ready for a female coach right now.” It’s unclear if Staley would still consider a move to the NBA after that experience. Ujiri interviewed Hammon — the head coach of the WNBA’s Las Vegas Aces — for the Raptors job three years ago. One NBA executive told The Athletic that it wouldn’t be shocking if the Mavericks wanted to interview Staley.

item.title: Rival executives monitoring Kyrie Irving's long-term fit alongside Cooper Flagg
item.description: “Durant once told me that there’s only one Kyrie Irving walking around in the world,” Ujiri said most recently. “Yeah, I think we have to figure out a way how Kyrie fits with our program. I’ve had those conversations with Kyrie. I think Kyrie will fit. There’s a huge curiosity in our minds to see how Kyrie fits playing with Cooper Flagg. He’s just that kind of like an incredible talent and player. I think we owe this organization that.” Yet, Ujiri’s adjoining stance that Flagg and Irving can for

item.title: Mike Schmitz on Kyrie Irving and Cooper Flagg: 'It's a match made in Heaven'
item.description: Notwithstanding, new Mavericks general manager Mike Schmitz echoed Ujiri’s sentiment and lauded the pairing of Cooper Flagg and Irving on paper when looking ahead to the upcoming season. “That’s something you dream of,” Schmitz said. “Having a magician with the ball like that who can pass, dribble, shoot, and someone with the connective qualities of Cooper. It’s a match made in Heaven.”

item.title: Thus, while the stars may not align on paper for a …
item.description: It’s worth noting that former Dallas Mavericks head coach Jason Kidd has quietly been connected to Portland within league circles. Yet, will that chatter lead to anything more substantive now or in the future? Now, Portland’s current price range for a head coach is well below Kidd’s remaining contract with the Mavericks, which is for four years and $40 million, according to ESPN. Kidd also expressed a desire to make front office personnel decisions before Dallas hired Masai Ujiri and Portland g

item.title: Lakers and Mavericks interested in 76ers’ Prosper Karangwa
item.description: While Nelson appears to be the safest of any current 76ers executive on staff, it’s unclear what’s next for others on the staff. For example, Prosper Karangwa has drawn interest from the Lakers and Mavericks, league sources told HoopsHype.

item.title: Mavericks interested in adding Patrick Englebrecht, Prosper Karangwa to their front office
item.description: According to league sources, the Mavericks have expressed interest in adding Philadelphia’s Prosper Karangwa to their front office, and Toronto’s director of global scouting, Patrick Englebrecht, is also a candidate to reunite with Ujiri in Dallas after they worked together since the 2013-14 season.

item.title: Charlie Villanueva doesn't have Shai Gilgeous-Alexander in his Top 10 point guards ever
item.description: Charlie Villanueva on Shai Gilgeous-Alexander: I wouldn't even put him in the top 10. Q. Give me 10 point guards, combo point guards, it doesn’t matter. Villanueva: Magic Johnson, Isiah Thomas, Stephen Curry, Allen Iverson, John Stockton… I wouldn't put him up past John Stockton, fam. Q. But that's only five you've named so far. Villanueva: Okay, let me get there! Steve Nash, Chris Paul… Okay. I'm cool. I'm cool with all these… Oh, also Tony Parker. (…) He’s not in front of Jason Kidd. (…) Bob C

item.title: Dallas a long-time intriguing destination for Giannis Antetokounmpo
item.description: League sources tell The Stein Line that there is at least one destination in the West that has long intrigued Giannis Antetokounmpo but which rarely gets mentioned. Dallas. The interest began to bubble years ago, of course, thanks to the presence of the Luka Dončić in The 214 and the tantalizing scenario of Giannis teaming up with Luka.

item.title: I’m told that the Mavericks actually did do some …
item.description: Antetokounmpo and Kidd have remained close even though they haven't worked together since the Bucks fired Kidd in January 2018 ... and I’m told that the Mavericks actually did do some behind-the-scenes investigating leading up to the February trade deadline to try to find out if they still appeal to the two-time MVP.

item.title: Kyrie Irving to Lakers not likely to happen?
item.description: As far as the Los Angeles Lakers' chances of pulling a potential deal around Irving, Fischer dismissed speculations that the franchise will try to reunite the star point guard with Luka Doncic in the wake of Austin Reaves' free-agency situation. "My only real understanding of what the Lakers want to do from a timeline perspective is they want to bring in a talent that fits 27-year-old Luka Doncic's timeline. As great as that pairing of Luka and Kyrie was, I think Kyrie is a little bit redundant

item.title: Magic to hire Sean Sweeney as head coach
item.description: Michael Scotto: JUST IN: The Orlando Magic will hire Sean Sweeney as their head coach, league sources told @hoopshype. Sweeney was an associate head coach for the San Antonio Spurs. Prior, he was an assistant coach for the Dallas Mavericks, Detroit Pistons, Milwaukee Bucks, and Brooklyn Nets.

item.title: “I can say he’s been doing everything humanly possible …
item.description: Dallas Mavericks general manager Mike Schmitz said he has seen Kyrie Irving doing everything possible to return at full strength from the torn ACL that ended his 2025-26 season, citing the 34-year-old guard’s presence and approach inside the team’s practice facility this offseason. Schmitz made the comments Friday during a roundtable with reporters at the American Airlines Center. “I can say he’s been doing everything humanly possible to maximize his talent and to do that,” Schmitz said. “Just t

item.title: Luka Doncic, Donnie Nelson acquire Italian club to submit NBA Europe bid
item.description: Did he want to help move a professional basketball team to Rome? The question came from Donnie Nelson, a former Mavericks executive who had brought Mr. Doncic to the National Basketball Association from Europe and was among those celebrating the win. Mr. Doncic barely had to think before answering yes. The pair are now the lead decision makers of an investment group that has bought a northern Italian basketball team, Vanoli Cremona, from a town that is famous for its violin makers. The group pla

item.title: “My mom, when she immigrated here, she understood the …
item.description: A New Jersey native, Towns also explained why he chose the Knicks over the Nets. “My mom, when she immigrated here, she understood the magnitude of Madison Square Garden,” Towns said. “… I grew up, obviously, in Jersey, obviously it was so much easier for me to get New Jersey Nets tickets and see Kenyon Martin, Vince Carter, Jason Kidd, but my family has always had such respect and love for the Knicks and my father having ties to the Knicks, it was kind of guaranteed I was going to fall into thi

item.title: Kyrie Irving says he is close to 100 percent in his ACL recovery
item.description: Mike Curtis: Kyrie Irving: “I am definitely close to being over at 100% in terms of my ACL recovery. It’s been a while now…I’m so grateful that I’ve had the time to heal & just experiment with my body more on the court and off the court in the weight room & just pushing myself to the limit.”

item.title: "Mike Brown at best was the sixth choice for this job.
item.description: Marc Stein: "Mike Brown at best was the sixth choice for this job. Remember that the Knicks asked for permission to speak to Jason Kidd, Ime Udoka, Quinn Snyder, Chris Finch, and Billy Donovan. All of those teams said no. Jason Kidd and Billy Donovan both got contract extensions and no longer coach the teams where they were a year ago. It really looked like the Knicks fired Tom Thibodeau without a plan to replace the first coach who had taken them to the conference finals this century. For thing

item.title: "Who are your top three defensive teammates of all …
item.description: Draymond Green: "Who are your top three defensive teammates of all time? Andrew Bogut number one, Klay Thompson number two. Klay Thompson, ball hawk. And I'd say number three, number three, my favorite defensive teammate of all time, or not favorite, but top three. Kevin Durant was elite defensively here. Elite. Andre Iguodala. Andre Iguodala made the game really easy. Got to go with Andre. It has to be Andre."

item.title: Derek Harper expects Luka Doncic to retire with the Mavericks
item.description: Derek Harper: "I love Nico, Nico is my buddy, but I would not want my name on that trade if I ever had anything to do with the NBA. You look back, you gave away what, 30 points, eight and nine, you gave it to somebody else for a guy that is no longer on your roster. That one is hard to get around. It is just something at the time I think they thought about doing and they were quick to react, and it just did not work out, especially here in Dallas. People love Luka here. I think he will eventuall

item.title: NBAers in for Canada this summer:
item.description: Josh Lewenberg: To sum up NBAers in for Canada this summer: SGA, NAW, Barrett, Brooks, Dort, Nembhard bros, Mathurin, Olynyk, George, Miller Out this summer but in for this cycle: Edey, Powell Didn’t commit: Murray, Sharpe, Wiggins Committed but not selected: Lawson, Banton, Houstan, Prosper

item.title: After more than a year of analyzing Dallas sites for …
item.description: After more than a year of analyzing Dallas sites for their planned multi-billion-dollar arena and entertainment district, the Mavericks have chosen Valley View, dealing a major setback to efforts to bring the project downtown. The Mavericks on Monday executed option agreements to purchase approximately 104 acres of the 110-acre former site of Valley View Mall, the franchise told The Dallas Morning News in an emailed statement.

item.title: Multiple people close to Mavericks governor Dumont say …
item.description: Multiple people close to Mavericks governor Dumont say he grew increasingly leery of becoming the face of a tear-down-City Hall movement, especially since it wasn’t the Mavericks’ idea in the first place. Along with availability, multiple people close to negotiations said the Mavericks had logistical concerns about the City Hall site’s ingress and egress on game and concert nights.

item.title: There is a growing anticipation that Prosper Karangwa …
item.description: There is a growing anticipation that Prosper Karangwa will elect to stay with the 76ers after the hiring of Mike Gansey as the team's new president of basketball operations. Karangwa is the Sixers' vice president of player personnel and has drawn interest in recent weeks from the Los Angeles Lakers and Dallas.

item.title: The Mavericks have chosen Valley View, dealing a major …
item.description: After more than a year of analyzing Dallas sites for their planned multi-billion-dollar arena and entertainment district, the Mavericks have chosen Valley View, dealing a major setback to efforts to bring the project downtown. The Mavericks on Monday executed option agreements to purchase approximately 104 acres of the 110-acre former site of Valley View Mall, the franchise told The Dallas Morning News in an emailed statement. It’s a seismic decision that could reshape Dallas’ sports and enterta

item.title: That timeline was a significant factor in Monday’s …
item.description: The Mavericks were seeking at least a 50-acre footprint for the project, the centerpiece being a basketball-specific arena to be completed by the time its American Airlines Center lease expires on July 28, 2031. That timeline was a significant factor in Monday’s decision, multiple people familiar with the negotiating process told The News.

item.title: Dereck Lively: "My mom is the first person, first …
item.description: Dereck Lively: "My mom is the first person, first coach. She used to coach me to kick me out of a gym. She kicked me out of practice because I called her mom, and then I had to sit the entire rest of practice outside, couldn't get in the car, I was locked out of the gym, and I just had to sit on the curb. She was a loving person, but when it came to basketball, she knew everything around the game. She taught me many things, no matter if it's, as soon as you start a commitment, you got to finish

item.title: Kyrie Irving's time in Dallas running out?
item.description: In his latest Bleacher Report livestream, NBA insider Jake Fischer argued that he does not fully expect the star point guard to remain in Dallas into the next 2026-27 season. “I don’t necessarily believe that Kyrie Irving will be on the Mavericks roster come opening night," Fischer said. "There will be contending teams that would make trade calls to Dallas.”

item.title: On Wednesday, Obi Agbim, Michael Ajayi, Christian …
item.description: Dallas enters the June 23-24 draft with three picks: Nos. 9, 30 and 48. On Wednesday, Obi Agbim, Michael Ajayi, Christian Anderson, Tucker DeVries and Wyatt Fricks became the latest players to work out at the Mavericks’ practice facility, Agbim told The Dallas Morning News.

item.title: Marshall is close friends with Knicks guard Jose …
item.description: Mike Curtis: #Mavs forward Naji Marshall is in San Antonio for tonight’s Game 1 between the #Knicks and #Spurs. Marshall is close friends with Knicks guard Jose Alvarado, his former teammate of three seasons in New Orleans.

item.title: “And then, obviously, Nico is talking trash behind the …
item.description: Mark Cuban: “I mean, like, me going to Patrick: ‘Okay, Patrick, what’s going on?’ “He’s going to give you a report, right? Every two weeks, you get a report, and you sit down. Then, ‘Oh, yeah, I want you to talk more with Nico. Get down there with Nico.’ “And then, obviously, Nico is talking trash behind the scenes. But again, we’re winning. I don’t want to create a problem when things are going well. “And if we had been losing, I probably would have stepped forward and said, ‘Look, this is not

item.title: Mark Cuban accuses Nico Harrison of taking credit for the Kyrie Irving deal
item.description: Interviewer: “So, when you say ‘talking trash behind the scenes,’ like politically maneuvering? Going to maybe Patrick and saying, ‘Hey, I’m the guy doing this, not Mark’?” Mark Cuban: “Absolutely.” Interviewer: “What is he doing to you? What is he saying? How do you find out about—” Mark Cuban: “Like, ‘Oh, I did the Kyrie deal. He didn’t do the Kyrie deal.’ “Right. Yeah, he might have talked to the general manager, but I’m the one that made the decision. I talked to the agent. I talked to the o

item.title: Mark Cuban: "Masai and Nico are a lot alike, but …
item.description: Mark Cuban: "Masai and Nico are a lot alike, but they're different in the important ways. Nico wanted all his people in there, and you saw how many people he got rid of and who he got rid of. Masai's a lot of the same way. But Nico didn't really know what he was doing. I hired him to work for me, right, not to run the the the basketball operations."

item.title: "I had gotten a Tesla and I said something that I …
item.description: Mark Cuban on Elon Musk: "I had gotten a Tesla and I said something that I didn't like about it. And he just texted me and said, 'Fuck you.'"

item.title: Mavs to interview Terry Stotts for head coaching job
item.description: The Stein Line has also been advised that former Trail Blazers, Bucks and Hawks coach Terry Stotts — who previously had a stint in Dallas as an assistant and was once recommended strongly for Kidd's staff by former majority owner Mark Cuban — is also certain to get an interview for the position.

item.title: Those include Minnesota's Micah Nori, Houston's Royal …
item.description: Yet the more realistic outcome for Dallas remains hiring someone from within the NBA and, to that end, sources say that the Mavericks have either already registered official interest or plan to do so with a number of prominent assistant coaches. Those include Minnesota's Micah Nori, Houston's Royal Ivey, Toronto's Jama Mahlalela, Boston's Tony Dobbins and Miami consultant Noah LaRoche.

item.title: “I mean, I haven’t heard anything really from Dallas,” …
item.description: Minnesota Timberwolves assistant coach Micah Nori, regarded as one of the most coveted names on this year’s head coaching market, said he has had no contact with the Dallas Mavericks about their vacancy. Nori addressed the Mavericks’ opening along with other searches around the league during an appearance on SiriusXM’s NBA Radio on Tuesday. “I mean, I haven’t heard anything really from Dallas,” Nori said.

item.title: “It is a business. I’m not necessarily surprised when …
item.description: Mike Curtis: Asked Daniel Gafford for his impressions of Masai Ujiri & the decisions he’s made over the last month. “It is a business. I’m not necessarily surprised when it comes to some of the things that he’s done with the organization because I’m pretty sure he already had that in his back pocket when he got hired. I’m just sitting back & watching from afar…I’ve yet to get the chance to sit down & talk with him, but he made sure he called me & we had a good conversation on the phone.”

item.title: “I’m getting there. The rehab is good. I’m staying …
item.description: Mike Curtis: Daniel Gafford was asked if he feels 100% healthy: “I’m getting there. The rehab is good. I’m staying consistent. I’m holding myself accountable to just take care of my body…I don’t want to rush anything. Whenever I get back on the court & do all the workouts, I want to be 100% healthy.”

item.title: The franchise’s longtime partnership with Chime has …
item.description: The Dallas Mavericks are in need of a new jersey patch sponsor. The franchise’s longtime partnership with Chime has ended after seven years, a team spokesperson confirmed Friday. The team celebrated the end of the sponsorship with the popular financial services company with a tribute video on social media.

item.title: “Seven years of Mavs x Chime. An unforgettable run …
item.description: “Seven years of Mavs x Chime. An unforgettable run defined by positive impact for fans and local communities, rooted in financial empowerment,” the Mavericks said in a post on X. “Together, @Chime and @dallasmavs built something that really mattered. "As we turn the page, we're grateful for everything we accomplished together and will be cheering each other on for years to come! 💚💙”

item.title: Jalen Brunson camp 'laughed' at Mavericks after four-year, $55 million extension offer
item.description: NBA Base: The Jalen Brunson camp reportedly "laughed" at the Mavericks when Dallas tried to offer him a 4-year, $55M extension near the 2022 trade deadline, per @TheSteinLine "The Brunson camp basically laughed at them and said, 'Uh, we'll take our chances in free agency now, you guys had your chance.' ... The Mavs still had the option at that point of picking up the phone and saying, 'Jalen, we completely screwed this up, we are deeply sorry, we've disrespected you, we want to make this right,

item.title: Nick Van Exel says Kobe Bryant outworked Dirk Nowitzki and Manu Ginobili: 'It wasn’t even close'
item.description: Nick Van Exel: "I've been around some incredible workers. I played with Manu Ginobili. I played with Dirk Nowitzki. Those guys were among the hardest workers I've ever seen in basketball. But Kobe Bryant was on another level. When it came to work ethic, he ran laps around people. It wasn't even close. That's not disrespect toward Manu or Dirk. It's a compliment to Kobe. His commitment was extreme. His attention to detail was obsessive. Even as an 18-year-old, he approached improvement differentl

item.title: Burries is the name I hear the most with Dallas, …
item.description: Burries is the name I hear the most with Dallas, though, with sources around the lottery trying to determine why he’s not working out in as many places as you’d expect given his standing at the start of the process. There is a lot of enthusiasm for Burries among teams who want to try to win next season, as he’s seen as a tough guard who can dribble, pass and shoot who will also defend at a high level with physicality.

item.title: Indications are strong that he's not looking to leave …
item.description: In Scheyer's case, for the record, indications are strong that he's not looking to leave Duke for the pros ... even if it would mean a reunion with Mavericks star Cooper Flagg. Mike Krzyzewski's hand-picked successor as Blue Devils coach just announced two additions to his staff earlier this week.

item.title: The Brooklyn Nets and the Milwaukee Bucks have …
item.description: James Barlowe: Sources: The Brooklyn Nets and the Milwaukee Bucks have recently both visited Mikel Brown Jr in Orlando to watch workouts and meet with the family. Brown Jr is scheduled to visit the Atlanta Hawks, Brooklyn Nets, Los Angeles Clippers and Dallas Mavericks before the draft.

item.title: "All I know is he had the same things that Hardaway …
item.description: Now a player he thought compared to other unconventional all-time greats he had drafted -- Steve Nash and Tim Hardaway -- had somehow fallen to his team at No. 33. "All I know is he had the same things that Hardaway and Nash had -- heart, brains and balls," Nelson told ESPN. "Those are things that generally don't fit into an analytics model."

item.title: "I think Jalen had a loyalty to the Mavs because …
item.description: By that point, Rose had represented both Brunsons as an agent. When Leon left for the Knicks, son Sam Rose joined Jalen Brunson's representation team. In 2022, less than a month before Jalen Brunson was set to hit free agency, Leon Rose hired Rick Brunson as an assistant coach on Tom Thibodeau's staff. "I think Jalen had a loyalty to the Mavs because they'd drafted him, but the Knicks were his actual family," said a Mavericks source. "I don't think we fully grasped that."

item.title: #Mavs held predraft workouts today with a group …
item.description: Mike Curtis: The #Mavs held predraft workouts today with a group including the following players, per league source: Sam Alexis (Indiana) DJ Armstrong (UMBC) Quincy Ballard (Mississippi State) Kylan Boswell (Illinois) David Dixon (Duquesne) Mark Mitchell (Missouri)

item.title: With those two, one guy who's a freight train in …
item.description: Kevin Love: "I came in and thought I'll probably be the third guy, but I'm still going to be able to put up my fair share of numbers and play my game. With those two, one guy who's a freight train in LeBron James, another who is a magician with the basketball, the thing that I had to do in order for that team to thrive was space the floor."

item.title: I've never seen a Kyrie-led team win a title.
item.description: Paul Pierce: "This sparked up some conversation with me and some of my friends. I'm like, "Hey, is Jalen Brunson better than Kyrie all time if he wins?" Man, hold on now. I have to agree with that because I've never seen a Kyrie-led team win a title. And as great as Kyrie has been in his career, there's gonna be some tough conversations made if or when the Knicks win this title about Jalen Brunson and where he stands in NBA history as one of the better guards."

item.title: Mavericks star and reigning Rookie Of The Year Cooper …
item.description: Kevin Gray Jr.: Mavericks star and reigning Rookie Of The Year Cooper Flagg has been named to TIME’s inaugural 2026 TIME100 Sports list, recognizing 100 of the most influential figures shaping the global sports landscape. (📸: @TIME)

item.title: Though his teammate AJ Dybantsa has garnered more of …
item.description: Though his teammate AJ Dybantsa has garnered more of the headlines, as the projected No. 1 pick in the 2026 NBA draft, Keba Keita has consistently impressed NBA teams throughout the predraft process. “Keba is on the rise,” his agent, Keith Kreiter, said. “He’s been outplaying bigs who are projected to go in the first round. His physicality and ability to guard one through five makes him a no-brainer for the mid-to-late second round.” He’s worked out with the Utah Jazz, Washington Wizards, Charlo

item.title: “I don’t think I’ve seen a player at his age handle …
item.description: “I don’t think I’ve seen a player at his age handle the platform and pressure that he’s been able to, with the grace that he’s been able to,” said veteran Spurs forward Harrison Barnes, who won a ring with Curry in Golden State in 2015 and played alongside Dirk Nowitzki in Dallas. “He feels very natural in those moments,” Barnes said. “I think, coming into (Monday) night, in 14 years, that was one of the craziest atmospheres I’ve seen for a playoff game. Especially a finals game. I feel like he

item.title: Several teams could be interested in Kel'el Ware if he got traded to the Bucks and they wanted to flip him
item.description: Brian Windhorst: There'd be quite a few teams that would be interested in Kel'el Ware if he got shipped to Milwaukee and they were willing to flip him. Tim MacMahon: He's a rim-running floor stretching big. Windhorst: If I were the Lakers and Kel'el Ware got shipped to Milwaukee, I would be on the phone because Kel'el Ware package of talent is not that different from Dereck Lively.

item.title: The Mavericks, however, have told rival teams that …
item.description: Sources say that the Mavericks, however, have told rival teams that they are very much open for trades in either direction with their No. 9 pick.

item.title: Yaxel Lendeborg so far has worked out for the …
item.description: Dalton Johnson: Yaxel Lendeborg so far has worked out for the Warriors, Thunder, Heat, Bucks, Hornets and Mavs. He has workouts with the Clippers and Hawks left

item.title: Pistons, Timberwolves have interest in Kyrie Irving, Mavericks saying he's not available
item.description: The Mavericks, as we've been forecasting for some time in various appearances on the DLLS Mavs podcast, have indeed received trade inquiries for star guard Kyrie Irving, league sources say. However ... Sources tell The Stein Line that the Mavericks, thus far this offseason, have been messaging to teams that Irving is not available. Detroit and Minnesota are known to have Irving interest, but The Stein Line has learned that Houston — oft-mentioned as a potential suitor — has yet to make any overt

item.title: Mavericks expected to turn to rising NBA assistant for head coaching job
item.description: In the wake of Dallas moving on from Jason Kidd, there have been rumblings of both Duke's Jon Scheyer and Michigan's Dusty May being linked to the vacancy. But ever since Masai Ujiri was hired to run basketball operations last month, the belief around the league is that the most likely outcome for Dallas is elevating one of the NBA's rising assistants. That's what Ujiri did the other two times he hired a coach, both with the Toronto Raptors: In 2018, he replaced Dwane Casey with Nick Nurse. In 2

item.title: Jalen Brunson was asked during these finals what he …
item.description: Jalen Brunson was asked during these finals what he thought teams and scouts missed on him going through the draft process. With a smirk, he uttered one word: “Everything.” “I knew through college, but I really knew when he signed (with the Knicks),” Bridges, who played with Brunson at Villanova, said when asked when he realized his longtime friend would become an NBA star. “I knew what he was going to do, especially in the league we play in. Him having the ball and being able to be ball dominan

item.title: As one person with the organization defined the term, …
item.description: The scout realized a trait of Brunson’s that day, one that then-Mavericks general manager Donnie Nelson already valued when he insisted on plucking Jalen Brunson with that 33rd overall pick. Brunson, he told colleagues, had “gold dust” on him. The descriptor made its way around the Mavs scouting department. As one person with the organization defined the term, “He just f—ing wins.” Brunson, as plenty around him put it, was covered in gold dust. Especially these days, with a 3-1 lead in the NBA F

item.title: Knicks fans were heaving half-court shots to reach San …
item.description: Knicks fans were heaving half-court shots to reach San Antonio ahead of New York’s potentially championship-clinching showdown with the Spurs after stormy weather canceled flights Friday. Orange and blue-clad New Yorkers were ready to invade the second-rate Texas city for Saturday night’s Game 5 of the NBA Finals, but travel plans were thrown into chaos thanks to a raging thunderstorm that swept through the tristate area. Brooklyn resident Toby Hyde was supposed to board a 5:33 p.m. flight to Sa

item.title: Jason Kidd: Congratulations to @jalenbrunson1
item.description: Jason Kidd: Congratulations to @jalenbrunson1 and the @nyknicks

item.title: Knicks outscored Spurs by just 12 points total in the …
item.description: Mike Vorkunov: Knicks outscored Spurs by just 12 points total in the NBA Finals — tied for closest finals ever for a series that lasted 5 or fewer games. Celtics were +12 over the Mavericks in the 2024 Finals but that was skewed by a 38-point loss in Game 4. This series was tight the whole way.

item.title: Shout out to my real ones. @jalenbrunson1 @PJPatton52 …
item.description: Casey Smith: Shout out to my real ones. @jalenbrunson1 @PJPatton52 @heathaaamau They said we weren’t a good fit for their culture. Said we weren’t good enough in our roles. Peter Patton, Heather Mau, Jalen. We did and will continue to do the work.

item.title: And Nelson, I'm told, was quietly interviewed by the …
item.description: That's the same title Donnie Nelson held with the Mavericks for nearly 20 years. And Nelson, I'm told, was quietly interviewed by the Raptors last summer after Ujiri's departure as part of a search they conducted in the buildup to announcing Webster as Ujiri’s full-time successor in August 2025. Former Raptors coach Dwane Casey, who currently holds an advisory role with Detroit, also featured in Toronto's search.

item.title: University of Houston guard Kingston Flemings was in …
item.description: Kevin Gray Jr.: University of Houston guard Kingston Flemings was in Dallas today to audition for the Mavericks before next week's NBA Draft, league sources tell @DLLS_Sports. The Mavs hold the No. 9 selection.

item.title: Kyrie Irving staying in Dallas after all?
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: But in the last week to 10 days, there have been more and more signs coming out of Dallas that Kyrie is going to stay there; that the Mavericks want Kyrie to play alongside Cooper Flagg; that Kyrie really enjoys being in Dallas; and that he wants to stay there. It did seem like he has found some kind of comfort and tranquility there, so that makes sense. So while I believe that, if the Wolves were going to trade for Kyrie Irving, yes, maybe it would be Julius Randle, a first-rou

item.title: Micah Nori among top contenders for Blazers, Mavericks' coaching vacancies
item.description: Marc Stein: Wolves assistant Micah Nori is a top candidate for both of the NBA's coaching vacancies, league sources say. Nori is a confirmed Portland finalist alongside Boston's Tyler Lashbrook and among Dallas' top contenders. Tiago Splitter just left the Blazers to take Chicago's job

item.title: Coaches under consideration in Dallas, sources say, …
item.description: Marc Stein: Coaches under consideration in Dallas, sources say, include Micah Nori, Houston's Royal Ivey and the experienced Terry Stotts. The emergence of other candidates has not been ruled out as a search for Jason Kidd's successor launched on May 20 progresses. More: tinyurl.com/5xcrpc3j

item.title: Bennett Stirtz tells me he will be attending the NBA …
item.description: Elliot Clough: NEW: Bennett Stirtz tells me he will be attending the NBA Draft at the Barclays Center in Brooklyn, New York next Tuesday. Coach Ben McCollum will join Stirtz and his family in the green room. So far, Stirtz has worked out with the following teams: Charlotte Hornets, Chicago Bulls, Dallas Mavericks, Memphis Grizzlies, Oklahoma City Thunder, Toronto Raptors. He'll also workout for the Golden State Warriors prior to the draft.

item.title: Dončić also praised former teammate Jalen Brunson, …
item.description: Dončić also praised former teammate Jalen Brunson, saying he had congratulated him after his strong postseason and also spoke about the possibility of what might have been if they both had stayed in Dallas. “He’s played really well. I sent him a message, and he has every reason to be happy. We’ve both improved a lot since then. We’re not the same players we used to be. It would have been very good, for sure.”

item.title: Mark Cuban: We didn’t see Jalen Brunson as what he would become
item.description: Mark Cuban has admitted why the Mavericks completely whiffed on letting Jalen Brunson walk to the Knicks. “It was really, really simple. We didn’t see JB, as what he would become,” Cuban, the owner of the Mavericks from 2000 to 2023, said in a recent appearance on “House of Haymaker.” Brunson spent four years with the Mavericks, averaging more than 15 points per game only in his final year, the 2021-22 season.

item.title: “He showed that star potential when Luka [Doncic] got …
item.description: “He showed that star potential when Luka [Doncic] got hurt, and he won those games against Utah for us, but we were trying to get a star to put next to Luka, and JB’s star had not risen yet,” Cuban said.

item.title: "I envisioned at some point I knew I was gonna win, …
item.description: As he was asked by Shaquille O'Neal in Inside the NBA if he thought he would be successful right now after playing with Doncic in the Dallas Mavericks, the newly-crowned NBA champion and Finals MVP can only be blunt about his response "I envisioned at some point I knew I was gonna win, that's just how my mindset it," he said. "I was gonna figure out what I had to do, what role I had to fill in order to win a championship. "Opportunity presented itself, and I didn't want for it to slip away."

item.title: Stirtz has worked out with the following teams:
item.description: Elliot Clough: Bennett Stirtz tells me he will be attending the NBA Draft at the Barclays Center in Brooklyn, New York next Tuesday. Coach Ben McCollum will join Stirtz and his family in the green room. So far, Stirtz has worked out with the following teams: Charlotte Hornets Chicago Bulls Dallas Mavericks Memphis Grizzlies Oklahoma City Thunder Toronto Raptors He'll also workout for the Golden State Warriors prior to the draft.

item.title: On the Mavericks' HC search: There is a persistent …
item.description: Marc Stein: On the Mavericks' HC search: There is a persistent belief in coaching circles that Toronto's Jama Mahlalela, along with Minnesota's Micah Nori and Houston's Royal Ivey as covered yesterday, continues to factor into Masai Ujiri's thinking if he opts to a hire a current assistant

item.title: As a Dallas contender along with Nori, Ivey and Terry …
item.description: Marc Stein: Mahlalela, first reported by @TheSteinLine as a Dallas contender along with Nori, Ivey and Terry Stotts on June 4, has worked the closest with Ujiri to date among known candidates for the post. TBD whether new names emerge.

item.title: Orlando Magic will add Riley Crean as an assistant …
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Orlando Magic will add Riley Crean as an assistant coach, league sources told @hoopshype. Crean was the head video coordinator of the Dallas Mavericks and spent three of four seasons there working with Orlando Magic coach Sean Sweeney. Riley, 27, is the son of coach Tom Crean

item.title: Sweeney often worked with the bigs, especially Dereck …
item.description: Grant Afseth: Riley Crean spent a lot of time learning from Sean Sweeney when they were both with the Dallas Mavericks. Sweeney often worked with the bigs, especially Dereck Lively II. After Sweeney joined the San Antonio Spurs, Crean often worked with the bigs.

item.title: I've since been told that a Milwaukee contingent …
item.description: For now, though, let's say that the Bucks do stay at No. 10. We've already written about Milwaukee's interest in the top guards on the board … everyone from Darius Acuff Jr. to Keaton Wagler to Mikel Brown Jr. after flying down to Orlando to meet with Brown. I've since been told that a Milwaukee contingent including general manager Jon Horst and new head coach Taylor Jenkins also traveled to New York recently to visit with Nate Ament during this pre-draft process. Now the Bucks are being looked

item.title: Mavericks not expected to trade Kyrie Irving
item.description: Marc J. Spears on Kyrie Irving: I do think he'll be back on the roster. I know their owner is really, really high on seeing him and Cooper Flagg play together. And then Masai Ujiri had said in his introductory press conference that he wanted to see that as well.

item.title: Pelicans have hired AJ Meyer as Director of Analytics
item.description: Shamit Dua: Pelicans have hired AJ Meyer as Director of Analytics. Meyer comes from the Suns with previous stints in Dallas and San Antonio.

item.title: The Mavericks continue to conjure a lot of speculation …
item.description: There’s not much new information out of Dallas since my previous mock draft. The Mavericks continue to conjure a lot of speculation among sources. They’ve explored trading this selection and are considering several different players and positions, from lead guards like Flemings, Wagler, Brown, Acuff and Christian Anderson to Ament to Yaxel Lendeborg.

item.title: Kyrie Irving and ANTA will launch a 2026 world tour …
item.description: Kyrie Irving and ANTA will launch a 2026 world tour beginning June 22 in Shanghai, with stops across China, Indonesia, and the United States, the brand announced Thursday. The tour, branded “The Return,” will move through Shanghai, Chengdu, Shenzhen, Jakarta, Las Vegas, New Jersey, and Macau between June 22 and October. It is Irving’s third major tour for the brand, where he serves as ANTA Basketball’s chief creative officer.

item.title: Nate Ament named four pre-draft workouts during a …
item.description: Former Tennessee forward Nate Ament named four pre-draft workouts during a SportsCenter interview, and the Dallas Mavericks were not on the list. The 6-foot-10 freshman said he had worked out for the Chicago Bulls, Brooklyn Nets, Oklahoma City Thunder, and Los Angeles Clippers ahead of Tuesday’s NBA Draft, where he is widely projected as a lottery pick after one season at Tennessee. The omission carries weight for Dallas, which holds the ninth pick and has been tied to interest in Ament. A team

item.title: Dallas does not appear among Ament’s stated workouts, …
item.description: The reporting that ties Dallas to Ament comes from Jake Fischer of The Stein Line, who listed the Los Angeles Clippers (No. 5), Brooklyn Nets (No. 6), and Mavericks (No. 9) among the teams that have shown interest, with some rival executives viewing the Milwaukee Bucks at No. 10 as Ament’s floor. Fischer also linked the New Orleans Pelicans, who do not own a lottery pick, as a team interested in trading to gain positioning to select Ament. Two of the four teams Ament named, Brooklyn and the Clip

item.title: Kevin Garnett: Victor Wembanyama should focus on core strength
item.description: Kevin Garnett on Victor Wembanyama: One thing I want to speak on is just Wemby's development. If I'm him, I’m working on core strength because when… Paul will tell you, I'm not the biggest guy, right? But he'll tell you when I sit down and post up, can you move me? Paul Pierce: No, he's strong. Garnett: I'm probably one of the strongest people you could ever run up on and look like how I look, but when it came down there to setting up on that block and then pitching and getting it off down there

item.title: “Bottom line, in the finals, it was kind of …
item.description: But the 2006 finals remains Dwyane Wade’s basketball masterpiece, one that saw him join Michael Jordan (1993), Rick Barry (1967) and Elgin Baylor (1962) as the only players with four consecutive 35-point games in the league’s championship series. From Games 3 through 6, Wade scored 16 more points in the paint than any Mavericks player. “Bottom line, in the finals, it was kind of Jordan-esque. It really was,” Mourning told The Athletic of Wade’s performance. “He averaged almost 40 points a game i

item.title: “All for one. One for all. WE completed the mission …
item.description: Legion Hoops: Kyrie on the 2016 Cavs reunion: “All for one. One for all. WE completed the mission together as brothers, and that's all that matters to me…”

item.title: Burries is the name I hear the most with Dallas, …
item.description: Burries is the name I hear the most with Dallas, though, with sources around the lottery trying to determine why he’s not working out in as many places as you’d expect. There’s a lot of enthusiasm for Burries among teams who want to try to win next season, as he’s seen as a tough guard who can dribble, pass and shoot who will also defend at a high level with physicality.

item.title: Mavericks are actively exploring trade options, league …
item.description: Marc Stein: Busy weekend in Dallas. The Mavericks are actively exploring trade options, league sources say, while also preparing for Nos. 9 and 30 in Tuesday's draft and progressing toward in a conclusion in their coaching search.

item.title: The Mavericks and Brayden Burries have mutual interest …
item.description: Dallas, though, has been very open, sources say, to moving down the board. We've been told the Mavericks and Brayden Burries have mutual interest at No. 9. But if Burries is not there — and we've already reported he's under legitimate consideration by the Clippers at No. 5 — that could ultimately lead to even more reason for Dallas to slide back.

item.title: One potential target if Dallas were to trade down: …
item.description: One potential target if Dallas were to trade down: Washington big man Hannes Steinbach. Sources say that Steinbach, whose father Burkhard played alongside Mavericks legend Dirk Nowitzki on the German League team in their shared hometown of Wurzburg some 30 years ago, is among the prospects to have worked out for the Mavericks under consideration for the No. 9 pick.

item.title: Love Island USA Season 8 has started, with audiences …
item.description: Love Island USA Season 8 has started, with audiences getting to know fresh faces in the Fiji setting — and the astute might recognize one name. Aniya Harvey is the daughter of retired NBA star Donnell Harvey, who played for the Dallas Mavericks, Orlando Magic, New Jersey Nets, Denver Nuggets and Phoenix Suns. Harvey made his final NBA appearance in 2005 but continued to play professionally internationally until 2014. According to Complex (1), Harvey has largely been out of the spotlight since he

item.title: Lewis also completed workouts this past week with the …
item.description: Lewis also completed workouts this past week with the Phoenix Suns, Dallas Mavericks, Minnesota Timberwolves, Boston Celtics, and Cleveland Cavaliers, sources said, ahead of the NBA Draft, which takes place over June 24 and 25 (AEDT) at the Barclays Centre in Brooklyn, New York.

item.title: Dallas and Oklahoma City, amid the Mavericks' myriad …
item.description: Also in that same draft range but not seamlessly connected to the primary topic at hand: The Stein Line has learned that Dallas and Oklahoma City, amid the Mavericks' myriad trade discussions throughout the weekend, have discussed a potential swap of No. 9 to the Thunder for the Nos. 12 and 17 picks.

item.title: The list of veterans whom the Wolves have discussed …
item.description: The list of veterans whom the Wolves have discussed this spring includes the Milwaukee Bucks’ Giannis Antetokounmpo, the Dallas Mavericks’ Kyrie Irving, the New Orleans Pelicans’ Trey Murphy III, the Chicago Bulls’ Josh Giddey, the Boston Celtics’ Derrick White and many more. A couple of important caveats are that every team in the league is considering veteran help, and that having interest in a player does not mean any potential trades have ever been close.

item.title: "A lot. I think he arrived during my second season. …
item.description: Doncic notably highlighted Nikola Jokic and how he enjoys playing with him: "When I watch Nikola Jokic play and when I play against him I feel great. We joke a bit with each other but the way he's playing is phenomenal. He's playing incredible and does stuff that he sees before anyone else does." Jokic also used a chance to thank Boban Marjanovic for helping him accommodate himself in the US: "A lot. I think he arrived during my second season. Bobi is a great guy, we all know that. We were alway

item.title: "The Pistons have expressed interest in Kyrie Irving. …
item.description: Marc Stein: "The Pistons have expressed interest in Kyrie Irving. Dallas is telling teams Kyrie Irving is not available. The Pistons have expressed interest in Trey Murphy, but so have a zillion other teams, and New Orleans' threshold for trading Trey Murphy away is really, really high. So the Pistons have looked at other targets. Tyler Herro, if he ends up going to Milwaukee in a Giannis trade, we reported that Thursday night, we revealed that, that the Pistons have interest in Tyler Herro, but

item.title: “I seen the effect that it had on LeBron when Kyrie …
item.description: Boston Celtics All-Star Jaylen Brown has been the subject of rumors since his season ended, which could split as one half of the 2024 champion All-NBA tandem alongside Jayson Tatum, of which Rich Paul is warning the front office. While looking back on his client LeBron James' final season with the Cleveland Cavs, Paul, James' agent, reflected on how the loss of Kyrie Irving humbled LeBron. For Paul, it was a split between two All-Stars that could have been avoided if the two had spoken to one an

item.title: Michigan's Dusty May to coach the Mavericks
item.description: Michigan coach Dusty May is finalizing a deal to take the Dallas Mavericks head coaching job, sources told ESPN, an axis-shifting move that significantly alters the college and NBA landscape. May, 49, led Michigan basketball to the national title in 2026. He'll coach a franchise with Cooper Flagg as a linchpin piece. The Mavericks fired Jason Kidd after a 26-56 season last year.

item.title: Knicks assistant Chris Jent getting real consideration for Blazers' head coaching job
item.description: Marc Stein: Dallas’ hiring of Dusty May leaves Portland as the NBA’s only team without a head coach entering Tuesday’s draft … with sources saying Knicks assistant Chris Jent is getting real consideration for that post alongside finalists Micah Nori (Wolves) and Tyler Lashbrook (Celtics).

item.title: Mavericks are hiring Dusty May as their next head …
item.description: Christian Clark: I can confirm the Mavericks are hiring Dusty May as their next head coach. A lot of talk throughout the process that Dallas was looking closely at the college ranks in its search. Mavs find Jason Kidd's successor more than one month after his firing.

item.title: Dusty May is the first college head coach to take an …
item.description: Dusty May is the first college head coach to take an NBA job since former Michigan coach John Beilein took the Cleveland Cavaliers job in 2019. The last to leave college immediately after winning a national title was Kansas' Larry Brown in 1988. Florida's Billy Donovan was the most recent NCAA title-winning coach to leave for the NBA in 2015.

item.title: During Michigan's championship parade in April, …
item.description: During Michigan's championship parade in April, athletic director Warde Manuel announced May had agreed to a new deal that would keep him at Michigan for "many years to come." That deal wasn't signed at the time of Manuel's proclamation, and no deal announcement emerged in the subsequent months.

item.title: Mark Cuban on the Mavericks' hiring of Michigan coach …
item.description: Mike Curtis: Mark Cuban on the Mavericks' hiring of Michigan coach Dusty May: "He is an Indiana grad. I met him way back when he worked as a student manager and video coach. I'm sure he will do a great job."

item.title: Dusty May's chance to coach Cooper Flagg among main reasons to take Mavs job
item.description: Jeff Goodman: One source very close to Dusty May on the main reason(s) he left Michigan for the NBA: 1) "The state of college basketball and not knowing what it's going to look like tomorrow, in a month or in 5 years." 2) "The organization and the chance to coach Cooper Flagg."

item.title: Yaxel Lendeborg said he fell down to his knees when he …
item.description: Chancellor Johnson: Yaxel Lendeborg said he fell down to his knees when he saw the Dusty May news. I asked him about his chances of joining his college coach in the pros: “I’m going to tell him he better pick me up, if he doesn’t I’m going to block him.. It would be amazing to stick with him.”

item.title: Kyrie Irving is kicking off his latest ANTA Tour this …
item.description: Nick DePaula: Kyrie Irving is kicking off his latest ANTA Tour this week — with a World Tour summer schedule featuring cities throughout Asia and in the US. The Las Vegas and New Jersey dates in July will also include Irving’s “Uncle Drew Circuit” youth basketball tournament games.

item.title: Those sources were granted anonymity so they could …
item.description: But NCAA and NBA sources who spoke with The Athletic consistently said they believe that May has what it takes to make the leap. Those sources were granted anonymity so they could speak freely, and “modern,” “collaborative” and “adaptable” were all words used to describe May, a Terre Haute, Ind., native. So was the word “kind,” which might seem strange for someone who got his start working as a student manager for the rough-edged Bob Knight at Indiana.

item.title: Duke’s Jon Scheyer was one name the Mavericks heavily …
item.description: The Mavericks interviewed NBA assistant coaches Micah Nori, Royal Ivey and Jama Mahlalelah, league sources said, while also closely examining the college ranks. Duke’s Jon Scheyer was one name the Mavericks heavily considered. In fact, their interest in him pre-dated Ujiri’s arrival, league sources said.

item.title: They have discussed a variety of trade-back scenarios, …
item.description: The Mavericks are expected to go into next season with Flagg and Kyrie Irving as cornerstones. They control the Nos. 9, 30 and 48 selections in this week’s NBA Draft. They have discussed a variety of trade-back scenarios, including one, according to league sources, that would net Dallas the No. 16 pick that Memphis currently controls.

item.title: Kyrie Irving calls reunion speculation 'psychotic' after missing 2016 Cavs gathering in Europe
item.description: Kyrie Irving: Me and my teammates who won a championship together in 2016. We are straight, y'all. All right, it's all love. Real talk. I'm actually walking through the streets of Shanghai right now. So, y'all just flow with me, man. Me and my teammates, me and my brothers who played on the Cavs, we are good. We are more than good. (…) Me and my teammates who won a championship together in 2016. We are straight, y'all. All right, it's all love. And when I get back to the States, I'm going to pop

item.title: I think had Steve Kerr not resigned in Golden State, …
item.description: Brian Windhorst: I think had Steve Kerr not resigned in Golden State, I think Dusty May would have been on the Warrior short list. I think he was on the short list a couple of other places and he was clearly on the short list for the Mavericks.

item.title: “One guy that I already heard attached to them is …
item.description: Heat Central: Khris Middleton has already been linked to the Miami Heat, per @WindhorstESPN. “One guy that I already heard attached to them is Khris Middleton. Who obviously has a long history with Giannis and Bobby Portis… I don’t think you’re looking at the Heat instantaneously as title contenders”

item.title: A lot of executives around the NBA believe that Dusty …
item.description: Shams Charania: "Think back to the last eight months. Patrick Dumont, the Mavericks owner, you let go of Nico Harrison and then bring in Masai Ujiri to run this organization, fire Jason Kidd within weeks after bringing in Masai Ujiri. Throughout the onset of the process, it was John Scheyer and Dusty May as the top two targets for the Dallas Mavericks. That's who they really zoned in on. A group of assistants were interviewed as well, but they landed on Dusty May. A lot of executives around the

item.title: "I'm going to tell him he better pick me up. If he …
item.description: Yaxel Lendeborg on Dusty May's hiring and potential reunion: "I'm going to tell him he better pick me up. If he doesn't, I'm going to be mad, I might block him. But it would be amazing being able to stick with him."

item.title: “The timelines, maybe looking back, were a little …
item.description: That plan was more than a decade in the making. He reached the first goal at the age of 32 — a little behind schedule. But when Dallas Mavericks team president Masai Ujiri hired him as the youngest general manager in the NBA on May 8, a few days before his 36th birthday, Schmitz was right on time for his dream job. “The timelines, maybe looking back, were a little arbitrary, round numbers,” Schmitz told The Dallas Morning News. “But I always believe in setting goals for yourself and focusing on

item.title: “Since I was a kid, I couldn’t sit still,” Schmitz …
item.description: This state of constant motion is where Schmitz is most comfortable. “Since I was a kid, I couldn’t sit still,” Schmitz told The News. “I was always running around the neighborhood with friends. I was the type of guy who’d be out in the Arizona heat for 18 hours a day, playing basketball. I always had a lot of energy as a human. I just enjoy doing things. … I’ve just never been a very static person. Obviously, there’s times where you want to slow down and kind of find your inner balance. I always

item.title: “We treated it like it was the NBA,” said Voigt, the …
item.description: Will Voigt was one of the first to witness Schmitz’s work ethic and keen sense for discovering talent. As the former head coach of the Bakersfield Jam, known in today’s G League as the Motor City Cruise, Voigt hired Schmitz fresh out of Arizona in 2012 as a “very low paid” video coordinator intern. While the role often entails creating and editing video for players and coaches to use to scout opponents, Schmitz was thrown into the deep end. “We treated it like it was the NBA,” said Voigt, the as

item.title: “I basically said to him, ‘Hey, I’m going to have 30 …
item.description: Schmitz spent just one year with the Jam from 2012-13, but used what he learned to position himself for the future. In 2012, he sent a cold email to DraftExpress founder Jonathan Givony, offering to make free player evaluation videos. “I basically said to him, ‘Hey, I’m going to have 30 videos on 30 prospects and if you want them, I’ll send them over to you for free.’ ” Schmitz told The News. “I think he heard ‘free’ and jumped all over it, and so that started that relationship.”

item.title: Sources say that the Hawks and Mavericks both visited …
item.description: Sources say that the Hawks and Mavericks both visited Ament last weekend in Memphis, where he was completing his pre-draft preparations.

item.title: #Mavs say new head coach Dusty May will be introduced …
item.description: Mike Curtis: The #Mavs say new head coach Dusty May will be introduced at an introductory press conference at a later date.

item.title: Michigan to receive $5 million as part of Dusty May's buyout
item.description: Jeff Goodman: Per Dusty May's Michigan contract, the school will receive $5 million as part of his buyout. May never signed a new contract after winning the national title, but did change agents - going from Andy Miller to CAA, source told @TheFieldOf68

item.title: The Dallas Mavericks today announced the appointment …
item.description: The Dallas Mavericks today announced the appointment of Dusty May as the franchise's new head coach. May joins the Mavericks after leading the University of Michigan to the 2026 NCAA National Championship. Widely regarded as one of the sport's premier program builders and player developers, May has earned a reputation for creating winning cultures, maximizing talent, and leading teams to sustained success.

item.title: "We set out to find a leader who embodies the values …
item.description: "We set out to find a leader who embodies the values we want to define our organization," said Dallas Mavericks President of Basketball Operations Masai Ujiri. "Dusty has won at every stage of his career because of his ability to build. He develops players, creates accountability, and brings people together around a shared standard of excellence. His work ethic is extraordinary, and his teams consistently reflect his values. When you study his journey, you see someone who has earned every opport

item.title: “I am honored to join the Dallas Mavericks …
item.description: “I am honored to join the Dallas Mavericks organization," said May. "This is one of the most respected franchises in professional sports, with passionate fans, a talented roster, and a clear commitment to building a championship organization. I am grateful to Patrick Dumont, Masai Ujiri, and the Mavericks organization for this opportunity, and I look forward to helping bring another championship to the city of Dallas."

item.title: "He's a guy who really cares for your well-being, not …
item.description: Mike Curtis: Michigan forward Yaxel Lendeborg on what Dusty May brings to the Mavs (via ESPN): "He's a guy who really cares for your well-being, not just what you can do for him on the court. He puts away all that stuff aside and steps in like a father-figure role and really cares."

item.title: He's gonna set the standard, but he's also going to …
item.description: Mike Curtis: Michigan forward Morez Johnson Jr. on what Dusty May brings to the Mavs (via ESPN): "He brings consistency. He's going to show up and be the same guy everyday. He's gonna set the standard, but he's also going to follow the standard as well. He's not just gonna say something & do the opposite. He's a great guy. He wants to put his players in the right positions to do well."

item.title: Masai Ujiri on hiring Dusty May: Many things translate from college to the pros
item.description: Mike Curtis: Asked Masai Ujiri about hiring Dusty May as Mavs coach: “Dusty stood out in many ways. I saw him in Chicago in the predraft camp & observed him. Talking to people & it’s a different ball game with NIL & college & how you operate with youth. It’s almost like a professional league in college. Many things translate.”

item.title: Dallas selects Morez Johnson Jr at No. 9
