item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The Dallas Mavericks will select Morez Johnson Jr. with the No. 9 pick in the 2026 NBA Draft.

item.title: He’s got to develop a consistent perimeter shot, and …
item.description: One college assistant coach identified a couple areas Morez Johnson Jr. must improve to make it as a pro. At the end of the year, he needed to finish his shots better rolling to the rim. Whether it was the length of people in the tournament or the competition being better, I don’t know. He’s got to develop a consistent perimeter shot, and they wanted him to take it. But the one thing this kid does is, he plays extremely hard. Runs the floor, is the point on their press. Guards multiple positions

item.title: Comps I’ve heard for Morez Johnson Jr., the Michigan …
item.description: Tim MacMahon: Comps I’ve heard for Morez Johnson Jr., the Michigan PF/C picked by the Mavs at No. 9: Bam Adebayo and Al Horford

item.title: Michigan Mavs now. I'm very grateful for this …
item.description: NBA on ESPN: “The Michigan Mavs now. I'm very grateful for this opportunity. I’m excited to do it with my coach.” Morez Johnson Jr. after he was selected No. 9 overall by Dallas, reuniting with Dusty May 💙

item.title: An absolute wow: With Morez Johnson Jr., Yaxel …
item.description: Matt Norlander‬: An absolute wow: With Morez Johnson Jr., Yaxel Lendeborg and Aday Mara being drafted, this marks the first time since 2007 that a team had three lottery picks, with none of those picks being a freshman. It was those championship Gators in 2007.

item.title: I've been playing against Coop for a very long time. …
item.description: Mike Curtis: Morez Johnson Jr. on playing alongside Cooper Flagg: "I think it can get dangerous, us two on the court at the same time. I've been playing against Coop for a very long time. I'm very excited & happy that I'm on the same side as him. Get some wins together. Have I known him before the draft? Yeah, yeah. We're the same age. Played AAU & stuff like that."

item.title: Phoenix moves up to No. 30 to take Koa Peat via Dallas
item.description: Brett Siegel: This trade is done, sources told @ClutchPoints . The Suns are moving up to No. 30 from Dallas to take Koa Peat.

item.title: Knicks now hold picks 31, 47 (via Suns) and 55 in …
item.description: Marc Stein: The Knicks now hold picks 31, 47 (via Suns) and 55 in Wednesday's Round 2 and will add four more future second-rounders when the moves made tonight are official. Dallas and Phoenix say they can't discuss No. 25 Sergio De Larrea and No. 30 Koa Peat until the moves are finalized.

item.title: Mike Schmitz was asked if Dusty May had any say in the …
item.description: Mike Curtis: Mike Schmitz was asked if Dusty May had any say in the selection of Morez Johnson Jr., who May coached last season at Michigan: “Dusty gave us a great rundown on several prospects in the draft that he’s familiar with. Just to have that insight from his perspective is obviously very unique. He spoke the world of Morez, referenced him as an ‘everyday guy.’

item.title: However, Khris Middleton was considered the “Giannis …
item.description: However, Khris Middleton was considered the “Giannis whisperer,” a player who had the star’s utmost respect and could deliver hard criticism or honest thoughts about his game. And while Middleton (and the coaching staff) struggled to create cohesion alongside Antetokounmpo and Lillard, Middleton had inherently understood the on-court hierarchy. He may not have liked it, but he long ago decided he would not be the problem. Following his trade, Middleton, bitter, rented a car to drive silently fro

item.title: A Shanghai fan whose social media account takes the …
item.description: On Tuesday, a Shanghai fan whose social media account takes the name of the Dallas Mavericks star as well as his jersey number posted a video on a Chinese platform that sparked widespread envy in the comments section. In the clip, the 18-year-old fan introduces himself and claims that Irving is currently inside his home, before panning the camera to reveal the NBA star waving to the audience. Inside the home, Irving chatted with the fan as if he were a friend, comparing hand sizes and gifting hi

item.title: Morez Johnson Jr. will wear No. 14 for the Dallas …
item.description: Mike Curtis: Morez Johnson Jr. will wear No. 14 for the Dallas Mavericks, last worn by former two-way guard Miles Kelly last season.

item.title: If Memphis doesn’t trade tonight’s No. 32 pick, I’ve …
item.description: Jake Fischer: If Memphis doesn’t trade tonight’s No. 32 pick, I’ve heard they hold some interest in Aaron Nkrumah. But the Grizzlies are indeed seeking more future second-round draft capital. Memphis already netted five second-round picks by maneuvering down the board last night. The Grizzlies, sources say, also received a 2029 most favorable second of Detroit/New York/Milwaukee, a 2031 Dallas second, and a 2032 Detroit second, for sliding from No. 16 to No. 21.

item.title: I’m told that the many moving parts that landed Cam …
item.description: Marc Stein: I’m told that the many moving parts that landed Cam Carr with the Lakers at No. 24, Sergio De Larrea with the Mavericks at No. 25, Koa Peat with the Suns at No. 30 and netted the Knicks five second-round picks is tracking to take the form of a four-team TRADE once made official.

item.title: The Detroit Pistons traded the No. 21 pick (Karim …
item.description: Michael Scotto: FULL TRADE: The Detroit Pistons traded the No. 21 pick (Karim Lopez) and three-second round picks (a 2029 second that’s the second-best of Pistons, Bucks, Knicks, a 2031 Mavericks second, and a 2032 Pistons second) to the Memphis Grizzlies for pick 17 (Ebuka Okorie), per sources.

item.title: Sergio De Larrea's 2026-27 plans with Dallas still uncertain
item.description: Marc Stein: The pre-draft expectation, sources tell @TheSteinLine & @DLLS_Sports, was that Sergio De Larrea would likely spend next season in Europe again. Told that 2026-27 plans now that he's Dallas-bound -- including whether De Larrea plays summer league -- are all being worked through.

item.title: "They called me at 6 in the morning, but I was asleep. …
item.description: DAZN Espana: "They called me at 6 in the morning, but I was asleep. In a few hours, we'll talk and see what decision we make" 👀 Will Sergio de Larrea make it to Dallas? Is his immediate future in the NBA? He responds on DAZN after winning the Liga Endesa 👑 #LigaEndesaxDAZN 🏀

item.title: Warriors wanted Brayden Burries at No. 11
item.description: The expectation: The Bucks would take Nate Ament from Tennessee at 10. So when Atlanta took Houston guard Kingston Flemings at No. 8, and Dallas selected Michigan forward Morez Johnson Jr. at No. 9, the Warriors thought they had their guy. But having acquired a second lottery pick one day earlier, Milwaukee could be greedy. And it was. The Bucks selected the best player on the board at No. 10 — Arizona guard Brayden Burries — knowing they could get Ament at No. 13. And they did. That’s how close

item.title: The three picks they’re getting back are the three …
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: Source confirms the Pistons have traded Isaiah Stewart, their longest-tenured player, to the Grizzlies. The three picks they’re getting back are the three they traded last night (2029 2nd that’s the second-best of Pistons, Knicks and Bucks, a 2031 Mavs 2nd and 2032 Pistons 2nd)

item.title: Mavs draft Tobi Lawal at No. 48
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The Dallas Mavericks will select Tobi Lawal with the No. 48 pick in the 2026 NBA Draft.

item.title: Tobi Lawal wil be the Mavericks' selection at No. 48,
item.description: Jake Fischer: Tobi Lawal wil be the Mavericks' selection at No. 48, sources say.

item.title: Knicks select Tyler Nickel at No. 47 via trade with Mavs
item.description: Brett Siegel: The Dallas Mavericks are selecting Tyler Nickel with the 47th overall pick in the 2026 NBA Draft.

item.title: Mavs selects Vsevolod Ishchenko at No. 56 via trade with Lakers
item.description: Shams Charania: The Lakers and Mavericks have made a trade -- L.A. sends No. 56 Vsevolod Ishchenko to Dallas.

item.title: Mavericks sent cash to the Lakers for Vsevolod …
item.description: Shams Charania: The Mavericks sent cash to the Lakers for Vsevolod Ishchenko at 56, sources said.

item.title: Dallas Mavericks are trading for Vsevolod Ishchenko, …
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Dallas Mavericks are trading for Vsevolod Ishchenko, who was just selected by the Los Angeles Lakers with the 56th pick.

item.title: The Dallas Mavericks are still mapping out when …
item.description: The Dallas Mavericks are still mapping out when Spanish guard Sergio De Larrea will join the roster, general manager Mike Schmitz said, leaving open whether the 20-year-old reports for the 2026-27 season or remains overseas a year longer. Dallas acquired De Larrea’s draft rights in a draft-night trade with the New York Knicks, who selected him at No. 25 before routing the pick to the Mavericks. He is widely projected as a draft-and-stash prospect expected to stay with Valencia in the near term.

item.title: The #Mavs have officially acquired the draft rights to …
item.description: Mike Curtis: The #Mavs have officially acquired the draft rights to 56th overall pick Vsevolod Ishchenko in a trade with the #Lakers in exchange for cash considerations. Ishchenko, a 6-foot-8 wing, averaged 8.7 points and 4.7 rebounds on 46.3% from 3 for Russia’s Lokomotiv Kuban.

item.title: Dusty May left Michigan after two seasons and a …
item.description: Dusty May left Michigan after two seasons and a national championship for the NBA, in part, because of the uncertainties of NIL support and the transfer portal, Michigan president Domenico Grasso said Thursday at the university’s Regents meeting. Grasso addressed the Board of Regents and those gathered at the meeting after budgets were presented by various departments.

item.title: Coach May told me that among his reasons for leaving …
item.description: “Moving to the NBA is an opportunity for Dusty to further elevate his career. We wish him all best,” Grasso said. “His departure, however, is a bellwether of college athletics. Our current system is in dire need of clarity and equitable reform. Coach May told me that among his reasons for leaving were uncertainties and pressures involving the transfer portal and NIL support for student-athletes. He and I agree that the future of college sports is headed in the wrong direction.”

item.title: The player in question is Jeremiah Robinson-Earl, a …
item.description: Aris has already confirmed several important additions, including Nenad Dimitrijevic, E. J. Liddell, and Khem Birch, and according to multiple Greek media reports, the club has now targeted a new reinforcement from the United States. The player in question is Jeremiah Robinson-Earl, a member of the Dallas Mavericks, who spent most of the season in the G League playing for the Texas Legends.

item.title: He too, he used to pick on Mark Cuban, he used to pick …
item.description: Dan Gilbert: "David Stern was a phenomenal commissioner for 30 years. He built that league. But he too, he used to pick on Mark Cuban, he used to pick on me, he picked on the new guys."

item.title: “I can’t even say that. I’m just waiting to see that …
item.description: Mike Curtis: Dereck Lively II was asked if there was any target date for his return: “I can’t even say that. I’m just waiting to see that my foot’s good. Even when it’s good, I gotta wait even more to make sure I don’t have to do this again…If I gotta take the things that the fans & the public gotta say about me, “I’m brittle. I’m all that.” Cool. When I come back, don’t forget who I am.”

item.title: Dereck Lively on return: 'Whenever I come back, don't forget who I am'
item.description: “I’m just waiting to see that my foot’s good,” Lively said. “Even when it’s good, I’ve got to wait even more to make sure I don’t have to deal with this again. I’m never going to rush back. I’m never going to worry about that.” He has heard the durability questions and waved them off. “If I’ve got to take what the fans or the public have to say about me, that I’m brittle or all that, cool,” Lively said. “But whenever I come back, don’t forget who I am.”

item.title: NBA coaching community irate at circumstances of Micah Nori's hiring
item.description: Tim MacMahon: "Everybody in the coaching community loves Micah Nori and respects Micah Nori and thinks that he is ready and deserving of a head coaching job. I talked to several coaches who were absolutely irate about the circumstances, specifically the structure of this contract. This is Tom Dundon's work. This is that Stanley Cup winning, Dallas-based billionaire who, in his brief time in the NBA, has been notoriously cheap, frugal, doesn't travel two-way guys for the playoffs, has had layoffs

item.title: Mavs interested in adding Willie Green to Dusty May's staff
item.description: Marc Stein: Former Pelicans coach Willie Green, who has been pursued by Golden State to rejoin Steve Kerr's staff as an assistant, also has interest from Dallas as new Mavericks coach Dusty May assembles his first NBA staff, league sources say. Former Bucks coach Adrian Griffin and Heat consultant Noah LaRoche have likewise been mentioned as expected candidates for May's bench in Dallas. As @TheSteinLine previously reported, LaRoche was part of the monthlong interview process that eventually led

item.title: Several teams are expected to show interest in signing …
item.description: Several teams are expected to show interest in signing Williams as an unrestricted free agent, including the Phoenix Suns, Golden State Warriors, Boston Celtics, and the Mavericks, who want to retain him, league sources told HoopsHype. Dallas has Early Bird Rights on Williams entering free agency this summer.

item.title: Marvin Bagley expected to earn at least the taxpayer MLE
item.description: Bagley, 27, is expected to draw interest from several teams and command an average annual salary in the taxpayer mid-level exception range or slightly higher.

item.title: Nikola Jovic says Tyler Herro was in 'every single' Heat trade rumor
item.description: Nikola Jovic: ‘Ever since I arrived in Miami, I have been in trade rumors for Kevin Durant twice. Yes, twice for KD. For Kyrie Irving twice. For Damian Lillard, it was already a done deal because Lillard publicly stated that he wanted to come to Miami. It was like, 'I want Miami,' and that was it. For Lillard! And now most recently, I was in there for Giannis. I have been in seven or eight trade rumors already. Me and my brother Tyler Herro. I mean, my brother Tyler Herro, what he has endured, i

item.title: Jay Triano, who has coached both the Raptors and the …
item.description: Sources say Jay Triano, who has coached both the Raptors and the Suns in addition to numerous stops as an assistant and a lengthy stint as head coach of Canada's national team, is a leading candidate to join Micah Nori’s first coaching staff in Portland after working on Jason Kidd’s staff in Dallas last season. Nori’s first coaching job in the NBA came after working as an advance scout for more than a decade when Triano made him part of his Raptors coaching staff.

item.title: Heat linked to Khris Middleton
item.description: Other potential targets have not been confirmed, but Miami has been strongly linked to veteran NBA forward Khris Middleton, who played with soon-to-be-new-Heat-forward Giannis Antetekounmpo in Milwaukee.

item.title: During his rookie season, Cooper Flagg became the …
item.description: Crazy Stats: During his rookie season, Cooper Flagg became the youngest player in @NBA history to record games of 35, 40, 45, and 50 points, as well as the youngest player to record at least 10 assists in a game.

item.title: Mavs also interested in Cam Johnson
item.description: Rival teams consistently point to Nuggets forward Cam Johnson, acquired last summer in a deal that sent away Michael Porter Jr., as the most likely candidate to be dealt, in large part because the 30-year-old has just one season left on his deal at $23 million. Miami and Dallas are believed to be among the many interested teams.

item.title: Mavs discussed a trade package with Clippers for Kawhi Leonard
item.description: Mavericks president Masai Ujiri has interest in reuniting with Kawhi Leonard, multiple league sources with knowledge of Dallas’ thinking told The Athletic. The Mavericks and LA Clippers have discussed a deal that would send the seven-time All-Star Leonard to Dallas for a package that would include P.J. Washington, Klay Thompson and draft picks, league sources said.

item.title: League sources say that interest on the part of the …
item.description: League sources say that interest on the part of the Mavericks and Raptors is genuine, though. It’s just a matter of what Clippers owner Steve Ballmer wants to do.

item.title: Should also note, as I have before: in 13 years under …
item.description: Josh Lewenberg: Should also note, as I have before: in 13 years under this Masai Ujiri and now Bobby Webster-led front office, the Raptors have never moved multiple 1st-round picks in a trade. Don't expect them to do it for a 35-year-old Kawhi Leonard.

item.title: Mavericks plan to bring back Ryan Nembhard next season by exercising team option
item.description: Marc Stein: The Mavericks plan to exercise guard Ryan Nembhard's $2.2 million team option for next season before today's 5 PM ET deadline, league sources say.

item.title: New Mavericks coach Dusty May expects to lean on Kyrie Irving in NBA coaching debut
item.description: Ron Harrod Jr: Mavs coach Dusty May said that he will lean on Kyrie Irving as a first-time NBA head coach. May said Irving is like a Jazz musician.

item.title: Dusty May said that his coaching staff will have a …
item.description: Ron Harrod Jr: Dusty May said that his coaching staff will have a college flair, but he will hire coaches with NBA experience too.

item.title: Hey, gotta love Dusty May’s candor, at the end of his …
item.description: Brad Townsend: Hey, gotta love Dusty May’s candor, at the end of his lengthy list of reasons why Dallas “checked all the boxes” in deciding to jump to the NBA, he said with a smile: “No-tax state doesn’t hurt.”

item.title: Dallas Mavericks have extended a qualifying offer to …
item.description: Mike Curtis: The Dallas Mavericks have extended a qualifying offer to Moussa Cisse, making him a restricted free agent, a person familiar with the situation says. Cisse played on a two-way contract as a rookie last season and averaged 4.5 points, 5.7 rebounds & 1.2 blocks in 38 games.

item.title: Dusty May could have been an option for Warriors?
item.description: ESPN’s Brian Windhorst revealed on the Hoop Collective podcast that Dusty May -- the newly hired Dallas Mavericks head coach and reigning national championship-winning coach at Michigan -- would have been on the Warriors' coaching short list had Kerr chosen not to return. “I think had Steve Kerr not re-signed in Golden State, Dusty May would have been on the Warriors’ short list,” Windhorst said. “I think he was on the short list a couple of other places, and he was clearly on the short list for

item.title: Dusty May said he has already spoken with every player …
item.description: Dusty May said he has already spoken with every player on the Mavericks’ roster, including Kyrie Irving, who will return this fall after missing all of last season with a torn ACL. May called Irving “one of the greatest point guards to ever play the game” and likened him to a “jazz musician.” “How to earn his trust?” May said. “Be yourself and work like crazy for him every single day. Kyrie knows what it takes to win a championship. As a first-time NBA coach, I’m going to lean on him in a lot of

item.title: Mavericks have emerged as leading suitor for Marcus Sasser via trade
item.description: Marc Stein: The Mavericks have emerged a leading suitor to acquire Detroit's Marcus Sasser via trade, league sources tell @TheSteinLine .

item.title: Rockets expected to land Marcus Smart
item.description: The Houston Rockets have significant interest in free agent guard Marcus Smart and are expected to work out a deal in the three-year, $21 million range that would reunite him with head coach Ime Udoka, sources told Dallas Hoops Journal. Such a deal would fall within the taxpayer mid-level exception threshold.

item.title: On top of the full non-taxpayer MLE and a $20.3M TPE …
item.description: Marc Stein: On top of the full non-taxpayer MLE and a $20.3M TPE via the Anthony Davis trade, Dallas also generated a $6 million trade exception by trading away Jaden Hardy. Sasser's $5.2M salary for next season could slot into it.

item.title: Max Christie and Naji Marshall are changing their …
item.description: Mike Curtis: Max Christie and Naji Marshall are changing their jersey numbers, per a #Mavs spokesperson: Christie will switch from No. 00 to No. 0, previously worn by Dante Exum in 2025-26. Marshall is changing his number from No. 13 to No. 3, previously worn by Anthony Davis in 2025-26.

item.title: Khris Middleton remains a possibility for one of those …
item.description: Veteran Khris Middleton remains a possibility for one of those minimum contracts. Miami has respect for veteran point guard Gabe Vincent, who was on the Heat’s 2023 Finals team, but it’s undecided if the Heat will pursue this. Either way, adding another point guard remains a necessity.

item.title: First-round pick Sergio De Larrea to play Summer League with Mavs
item.description: Marc Stein: Mavericks first-round pick Sergio De Larrea, I’m told, is now indeed expected to play in summer league next week … while the broader topic of plans for next season continue to be worked through. We discuss next on @DLLS_Mavs!

item.title: Santi Aldama traded to Dallas
item.description: Shams Charania: The Memphis Grizzlies are trading Santi Aldama to the Dallas Mavericks for AJ Johnson, a protected 2030 Warriors first-round pick and two future second-round picks, sources tell ESPN.

item.title: Mavericks also acquire the draft rights to Tarik …
item.description: Shams Charania: The Mavericks also acquire the draft rights to Tarik Biberovic, sources said. The Warriors pick in 2030 is protected 1 to 20, then turns into a second if not conveyed.

item.title: The deal also allows the Grizzlies to preserve its …
item.description: Shams Charania: Dallas acquires a versatile forward as Memphis continues to stack draft capital. The deal also allows the Grizzlies to preserve its full $29 million trade exception from the Jaren Jackson Jr. trade, which otherwise would have been reduced by their earlier offseason dealings.

item.title: The $17M salary of Aldama goes into the $20.8M …
item.description: Bobby Marks: The $17M salary of Aldama goes into the $20.8M exception created from the Anthony Davis trade in February. Aldama has 2 years left: $17M and $17M (T) Johnson has 2 years left: $3.2M and $5.4M (T)

item.title: The Memphis Grizzlies will be able to take Isaiah …
item.description: The Memphis Grizzlies will be able to take Isaiah Stewart into the trade exception created by Santi Aldama being dealt to Dallas, which will allow a the Grizzlies to maintain their bigger $28 million trade exception from February's Jaren Jackson, Jr. trade. The Grizzlies could not take Stewart into their nontaxpayer midlevel exception even though his base salary of $15 million fits. That's because he also has $1 million in unlikely incentives that count toward that exception.

item.title: I could see the Isaiah Stewart, Isaiah Joe, John …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: I could see the Isaiah Stewart, Isaiah Joe, John Collins, & Santi Aldama trades expand into a five-team trade between the Pistons, Grizzlies, Clippers, Mavericks, & Thunder. Pistons need to send out $20M to match for Collins & Joe. Stewart ($15M) & Marcus Sasser ($5.2M) work.

item.title: Dallas Mavericks are trading AJ Johnson, a protected …
item.description: Mike Curtis: The Dallas Mavericks are trading AJ Johnson, a protected 2030 first-round pick (via Warriors) & two future second-round picks to the #Grizzlies in exchange for Santi Aldama & the draft rights to Tarik Biberovic, a person familiar with the deal confirms.

item.title: Willie Green expected to join Mavs as top assistant coach
item.description: Marc Stein: The Mavericks, I’m told, are expected to secure former Pelicans coach Willie Green as a top assistant for Dusty May’s first NBA coaching staff. The Warriors hoped to bring him back for a second stint on Steve Kerr’s staff but May’s jump to the NBA gave Green two strong options.

item.title: Marvin Bagley III has agreed to a one-year deal with …
item.description: Shams Charania: Free agent center Marvin Bagley III has agreed to a one-year deal with the Denver Nuggets, sources tell ESPN. A critical frontcourt addition for the Nuggets.

item.title: Bagley and his agents Shy Saee and Mike George of …
item.description: Shams Charania: Bagley and his agents Shy Saee and Mike George of Klutch Sports Group worked through a handful of offers in free agency and landed on a strong opportunity for a West contender in the next career stage of the former No. 2 overall pick.

item.title: Dusty May: 'I want us to be the best passing team in the NBA'
item.description: Dusty May: "On both sides of basketball, we are going to try to make sure that everything we do fits our players' strengths and hides their weaknesses. As far as offense, the unselfish piece, I want us to be the best passing team in the NBA. I want us to play with top-five pace and effort to get it up the court quickly before the defense gets set. And then defensively, to be incredibly competitive. A team that competes every second of every game."

item.title: It's increasingly unlikely Dwight Powell will return …
item.description: Todd Whitehead: With Santi Aldama heading to Dallas, it's increasingly unlikely Dwight Powell will return to the Mavs for a 13th season. Powell's departure (and the Giannis trade) will leave just 2 players from the 2014-15 season who have stayed on the same team since then.

item.title: Mavericks targeting Anfernee Simons
item.description: Marc Stein: The Mavericks have emerged as a suitor for Anfernee Simons, league sources say. As @JakeLFischer reported yesterday, Golden State and Miami have continued to pursue the free agent scoring guard who finished last season with Chicago.

item.title: Former WNBA All-Star Sue Wicks, Dallas Mavericks CEO …
item.description: NBA Communications: Former WNBA All-Star Sue Wicks, Dallas Mavericks CEO Rick Welts and NBA referee Billy Kennedy were inducted into the LGBTQ Sports Hall of Fame as part of the 2026 class.

item.title: Warriors, Heat and Nuggets among teams interested in Anfernee Simons before signing with Philadelphia
item.description: Michael Scotto: Anfernee Simons had interest from the Philadelphia 76ers, Golden State Warriors, Miami Heat, Denver Nuggets, Dallas Mavericks, and Indiana Pacers, league sources said. Simons ultimately agreed to a two-year, $12.3 million deal, including a player option, as ESPN first reported.

item.title: Tobias Harris says "urgency to win a championship" is why he joined Spurs
item.description: As for what factored ultimately in his decision to choose the Spurs, Harris told HoopsHype via text message, “The urgency to win a championship. It felt like both our goals aligned.”

item.title: The Mavericks will next week officially acquire the …
item.description: Marc Stein: Expanding on a subject we covered earlier today on @DLLS_Mavs: The Mavericks will next week officially acquire the draft rights to Tarik Biberović and have opened discussions with his representatives about potentially bringing the sharpshooter to Dallas as early as next season, league sources tell @TheSteinLine. Biberović stars for Turkish power Fenerbahçe and sources say his contract includes a $2 million buyout. The most that the Mavericks can contribute to a buyout is $900,000,

item.title: Cam Christie is switching from No. 12 to No. 0 for the …
item.description: Etienne Catalan: Cam Christie is switching from No. 12 to No. 0 for the #Clippers. Number last worn by Bradley Beal in 2026. #NBA Christie brothers back on wearing the same number - as Max switched to 0 for the #Mavs as well.

item.title: According to Eurohoops sources, the Dallas Mavericks …
item.description: Tarık Biberović is attracting serious NBA interest following another strong season with Fenerbahçe. According to Eurohoops sources, the Dallas Mavericks are expected to present the 24-year-old forward with a significant contract offer in the coming days after acquiring his NBA rights in Wednesday’s trade.

item.title: I understand it may have been about financial reasons …
item.description: You saw this [Jaylen Brown] news and you thought what?" Robert Parish: "Ouch. First of all, I hated to see Jaylen being moved. I understand it may have been about financial reasons for moving him. I’m sure moving that contract gave the Celtics some more maneuvering room to add other pieces or new pieces. Understand that. I still do not like it. When I first heard about it, the first thing I thought about was the Dallas Mavericks when they moved Luka [Doncic], just historically a bad move. And I

item.title: The Dallas Mavericks announced today that they have …
item.description: Mavs PR: The Dallas Mavericks announced today that they have signed ninth overall pick Morez Johnson Jr. Per team policy, terms of the deal were not disclosed.

item.title: After LeBron James’ parting with the Lakers, his agent …
item.description: DLLS Mavs: The Mavericks and LeBron James, together? James’ agent, Rich Paul, explains why Dallas is a “realistic destination” for James as he decides his next team. Marc Stein: After LeBron James’ parting with the Lakers, his agent Rich Paul says on his podcast with Max Kellerman that he has had some level of dialogue with all but two of the NBA’s other 29 teams.

item.title: Mavs looking to add Garrett Temple to Dusty May's coaching staff
item.description: Marc Stein: Also: @TheSteinLine has learned that new Mavericks coach Dusty May is also pursuing Garrett Temple for a spot on his first NBA coaching staff in the event that 16-year NBA veteran — who spent the past three seasons as a Toronto Raptor — elects to move into coaching.

item.title: The Dallas Mavericks announced their roster for the …
item.description: Mavs PR: The Dallas Mavericks announced their roster for the 2026 Las Vegas Summer League team today, headlined by 9th overall pick Morez Johnson Jr. Dallas tips off SL against Johnson’s former Michigan teammate Yaxel Lendeborg and the Golden State Warriors on July 9 at 6 p.m. CT.

item.title: Another hire for Dusty May's first NBA coaching staff: …
item.description: Marc Stein: Another hire for Dusty May's first NBA coaching staff: Joe Boylan. After stints with five other NBA teams, including Memphis, Boylan was initially expected to reunite with Taylor Jenkins on his new Milwaukee staff. But l’m told Boylan is now Dallas-bound along with Willie Green. Joe Boylan will coach the Mavericks’ summer league team.

item.title: Dallas Mavericks announced today that they have signed …
item.description: Mavs PR: The Dallas Mavericks announced today that they have signed first-round pick Sergio De Larrea. Per team policy, terms of the deal were not disclosed.

item.title: Mavericks Summer League team includes Ryan Nembhard, …
item.description: Christian Clark: Mavericks Summer League team includes Ryan Nembhard, Morez Johnson Jr., Sergio De Larrea, Vsevolod Ishchenko and John Poulakidas. Joe Boylan, who's had stops in New Orleans, Minnesota and Memphis, will coach the team.

item.title: Sergio De Larrea expected to play for Mavs at Summer League
item.description: Marc Stein: Source close to the talks tells @TheSteinLine regarding Spanish guard Sergio De Larrea coming directly to the NBA next season: "That is the plan." De Larrea plays for Dallas' summer league team in Las Vegas next week after helping Valencia win the Spanish ACB crown last week.

item.title: League sources tell @TheSteinLine and @DLLS_Sports …
item.description: Marc Stein: League sources tell @TheSteinLine and @DLLS_Sports that Spanish rookie Sergio De Larrea has signed a four-year, $16.3 million contract with the Mavericks at a full 120% of the rookie scale.

item.title: Dallas Mavericks announced today that they have signed …
item.description: Mavs PR: The Dallas Mavericks announced today that they have signed rookie Tobi Lawal to a Two-Way contract. Per team policy, terms of the deal were not disclosed.

item.title: For various reasons (injury/contractual, mostly), …
item.description: Josh Lewenberg: RJ Barrett, Zach Edey, Dwight Powell and Kelly Olynyk are here watching Canada-Puerto Rico. For various reasons (injury/contractual, mostly), they're not playing this summer but maintain spots in the country's player pool. Expected to be in the mix for the World Cup next summer.

item.title: “Well,” Brunson said with a pause. “They made …
item.description: SI noted that it saw people online saying Dallas and Nico Harrison “fumbled the two of you,” which prompted a smile from Brunson. “Well,” Brunson said with a pause. “They made decisions and I’ll leave it at that. They made their decisions.”

item.title: Garrett Temple retires after 16 NBA seasons, joins Mavericks coaching staff
item.description: Marc Stein: UPDATE: Garrett Temple is joining Dusty May's coaching staff in Dallas, Dan Eveloff of @PrioritySports confirms to @TheSteinLine. After a 16-year playing career, Temple has accepted May's pitch to move into coaching on a staff that also features ex-Pelicans coach Willie Green.

item.title: Bummer for the show but will be cool to see Garrett …
item.description: Blake Murphy: Bummer for the show but will be cool to see Garrett Temple’s next chapter play out. He had considered still playing, coaching, front office, and law in the past. Obviously has a ton to teach, now as a coach instead of teammate.

item.title: Braden Smith on undersized guards succeeding in NBA: It's kind of making a bit of a comeback
item.description: You see a guy like Jalen Brunson, leading his team to win the title. Throughout his career, people have talked about him being undersized. When you see that, does it tell you, ‘Hey guys, it doesn’t matter how big you are as long as you have the heart and can make the right reads, it really doesn’t matter what size you are’? Braden Smith: Yeah, that’s exactly what it is, and obviously, him doing that last year and with [Ryan] Nembhard on the Mavericks. I think with him playing as well as he did,

item.title: I just think that there are viable cases to be made …
item.description: Joe Vardon: "I told Rich that I thought this was the best or the most unique and that that had never been done before, what he did. It was absolutely incredible and unique, that entire message and the entire podcast, just to be so open about it. I think a lot of teams really are in the mix. I just think that there are viable cases to be made for Cleveland, Philadelphia, Miami, Golden State, and Dallas and the Timberwolves. I think those all those teams really are in play and I just feel like Ric

item.title: Tristan Thompson: 'If Kyrie Irving was two inches taller, he'd be the best player to ever touch a basketball'
item.description: Tristan Thompson: "If Kyrie Irving was two inches taller, he'd be the best player to ever touch a basketball on planet Earth. Because think about it, first of all, have you ever seen Kyrie Irving get a shot blocked? Never. And he's only what, 6'2, 6'3. But so imagine he was 6'5, what he could do right now. No, it would be other world. Like even if you ask LeBron James, like Kyrie Irving is probably the most talented. If you put Kyrie Irving in a workout, he could do anything. He has no flaws in

item.title: Tristan Thompson: "The reality is this, David Blatt …
item.description: Tristan Thompson: "The reality is this, David Blatt got hired to coach myself, Kyrie Irving, Dion Waiters, Tyler Zeller. He got hired before LeBron James signed back. So he got hired to help this young Cavs team mature to be a playoff team. He didn't get hired for LeBron James and Kevin Love to come in, you know, come be contenders. But the Cavs did a good job because they know David Blatt's coming from EuroLeague and he had multiple EuroLeague. So, you bring Tyronn Lue in and I think they the C

item.title: NBA GM on Celtics’ Jaylen Brown trade return: ‘That’s baffling to me’
item.description: Nobody was flabbergasted that the Boston Celtics made the decision to trade Jaylen Brown, the Finals MVP in that series over Doncic's Mavericks only two years ago, considering that he had been blatantly shopped for weeks. But to a bitter rival for that return? Eyebrows raised and jaws dropped across the league. "I mean, the guy got traded for less than Kessler," one general manager told ESPN, referring to the promising young big man who has yet to earn an All-Star bid. "That's baffling to me."

item.title: Last time a player signed an offer sheet of $5M+ per …
item.description: Bobby Marks: Some context on the Quinten Post offer sheet in Memphis. * Last time a player signed an offer sheet of $5M+ per season was Matisse Thybulle with Dallas in 2023. * Last time an offer sheet went unmatched was 2020 (Bogdan Bogdanovic with Sacramento/Atlanta)

item.title: @TheSteinLine that Rui Hachimura prioritized staying …
item.description: Marc Stein: League sources tell @TheSteinLine that Rui Hachimura prioritized staying in Los Angeles and chose the Clippers over interest from Brooklyn and Dallas as well as interest we previously reported from Golden State/Minnesota.

item.title: The Mavericks, who still possess the full $15 million …
item.description: Marc Stein: The Mavericks, who still possess the full $15 million midlevel, emerged as a suitor for Rui Hachimura along with the Clippers on top of Brooklyn's long-held interest ... but Hachimura had a clear intention to stay in LA after 3 1/2 seasons as a Laker.

item.title: Tarik Biberovic to join Mavericks on a multi-year deal
item.description: Fenerbahce Beko’s Tarık Biberovic has reached an agreement with the Dallas Mavericks on a multi-year contract to continue his career in the NBA, according to Eurohoops sources.

item.title: The official completion of this transition will …
item.description: The official completion of this transition will trigger a substantial buyout fee payable to Fener, allowing the player to officially make his long-awaited leap overseas. As he prepares to transition to North America, the Turkish club loses a core player but secures solid financial compensation.

item.title: DeMar DeRozan interested in Miami?
item.description: Barry Jackson: There has been no contact yet between DeRozan camp and Heat, per source just now. Miami would be among teams of interest to DeRozan, who is being set free by the Kings. Miami's options now include - among others - DeRozan, Beal, Gary Trent. Or the Heat simply could wait on LeBron decision before deciding what wing player to pursue. I expect Heat to try to add another wing; tough call is whether to pounce on one of these players or wait to see if a surprise name shakes free. Mavs -

item.title: "He's really good lob threat. That's what my game is …
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: Onyenso said his game is similar to Dereck Lively. "He's really good lob threat. That's what my game is like. And then what I do on defense."

item.title: The trade to make him a Maverick is still pending, but …
item.description: Marc Stein: UPDATE: The trade to make him a Maverick is still pending, but league sources say Tarik Biberović has informed Fenerbahçe he’s leaving for the NBA. After acquiring him from Memphis, Dallas is expected to sign Biberović to a two-year, $6 million deal with a team option in Year 2.

item.title: Dallas Hoops Journal has learned Tarik Biberovic is …
item.description: Dallas Hoops Journal has learned Tarik Biberovic is expected to leave Fenerbahçe and join the Mavericks once his buyout is finalized, ending an eight-season run in Turkey. Under NBA rules, Dallas can only cover $900,000 of the $2M buyout required for him to depart, leaving Biberović to cover the rest.

item.title: The Dallas Mavericks are expected to add one of …
item.description: The Dallas Mavericks are expected to add one of Europe’s top shooters. Dallas Hoops Journal has learned Tarik Biberović is expected to complete a buyout of his Fenerbahçe contract and join Dallas on a multi-year deal, ending an eight-season run in Turkey and setting up his arrival in the NBA this fall.

item.title: Dallas Hoops Journal has learned the two sides have …
item.description: Biberović signed a new three-year contract with Fenerbahçe on July 10, 2025, turning down interest from Memphis to stay in Istanbul. That deal carried a $2M buyout clause allowing him to leave for the NBA, and under league rules, Dallas could only contribute up to $900K toward it, leaving Biberović responsible for the rest. Dallas Hoops Journal has learned the two sides have worked through that gap, with Fenerbahçe likely to receive the full buyout amount to complete the move. That process s

item.title: Mavericks will create a $5.6 million trade exception …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: The Mavericks will create a $5.6 million trade exception by sign and trading Khris Middleton to these terms. The Wizards can absorb him into their $13.4 million trade exception which expires tomorrow.

item.title: Dallas is sending Middleton to the Wizards via …
item.description: Shams Charania: Dallas is sending Middleton to the Wizards via sign-and-trade as part of a larger six-team deal that includes, in total, the Mavericks, Wizards, Clippers, Pistons, Bucks and Grizzlies. It involves previously agreed upon deals such as John Collins, Gary Harris and Taurean Prince (Detroit), Isaiah Stewart (Memphis), Santi Aldama (Mavericks), Caris LeVert (Bucks), etc.

item.title: The Wizards have the $13.4M Kelly Olynyk trade …
item.description: Bobby Marks: Likely trade structure The Wizards have the $13.4M Kelly Olynyk trade exception that is set to expire on July 9. Deandrea Ayton- $8.1M Khris Middleton- $5.3M

item.title: Because of the Khris Middleton sign-and-trade, Dallas …
item.description: Bobby Marks: A nice job by the Mavericks front office on this deal. Because of the Khris Middleton sign-and-trade, Dallas will preserve the big $20.8M Anthony Davis trade exception. To break it down on a smaller scale: Middleton/AJ Johnson for Santi Aldama

item.title: One thing I learned about Khris Middleton from people …
item.description: Grant Afseth: One thing I learned about Khris Middleton from people with the Washington Wizards is just how valuable he was as a mentor. He proved to be a helpful presence for Cooper Flagg during his time in Dallas. It's easy to see why the Wizards wanted him back.

item.title: By helping Khris Middleton get to Washington via …
item.description: Marc Stein: By helping Khris Middleton get to Washington via sign-and-trade, Dallas can acquire Santi Aldama and Marcus Sasser without touching its $20.3M TPE from the Anthony Davis trade or its $15M MLE. The Mavs can then formally sign Tarik Biberović after the six-team trade is official.

item.title: Bucks get their own second-rounder in 2027 back from …
item.description: Eric Nehm: Update in here on the picks: - Bucks get their own second-rounder in 2027 back from the Pistons and then the least favorable of the Dallas Mavericks’ and Brooklyn Nets’ second-round picks in 2027 as well.

item.title: To an international scout this week who told me that …
item.description: KeithSmithNBA‬: Talked to an international scout this week who told me that new Mavs wing Tarik Biberovic is "the most plug-and-play Euro I've seen for the NBA in years. He's ready to be a rotation guy on Day 1 with his shooting." Biberovic, Aldama & Sasser, all while keeping the AD TPE fully intact is good stuff.

item.title: "Mitch Kupchak, he believed in me from the first day. …
item.description: Kai Jones: "Mitch Kupchak, he believed in me from the first day. He told me that he really thought I was going to be a great player in the NBA. And even when I was waived and a free agent, I called him up and I said, Mitch, thank you for everything you did for me. And he told me, I still think you have a chance to come back into the NBA and be a great player. And I think that I did, showing my preseason with the Clippers and my time with the Clippers and the Mavs. I think that I showed that I ca

item.title: I thought that was super cool because that was how I …
item.description: "Did you play with Cooper in the summer? Were you in those practices with USA Basketball?" LeBron James: "Yeah, he was on the development team, on the select team we call them. Yeah, and he played extremely well, man. I've been watching Cooper for a few years because my son played AAU basketball on the Nike EYBL circuit. Bronny, so we would be in tournaments either in Peach Jam in Georgia or Kansas City or Dallas or Indianapolis and Cooper, unlike a lot of kids, he played for his team out of Mai

item.title: LeBron James: "It just seems like he makes winning …
item.description: "Says something about his mentality. What did you notice practicing with him? What did you notice about his mentality?" LeBron James: "It just seems like he makes winning plays. He just makes winning plays. Even at his first year at Duke, he just made winning plays. Obviously, the fact that he was the freshman and player of the year this year as well, ACC player of the year, I just thought he played the game the right way. How you would hope that a player of his caliber just comes to work every

item.title: The Dallas Mavericks announced that Chase Bank will be …
item.description: The Dallas Mavericks announced that Chase Bank will be the official jersey patch partner. The team will hold a press conference at 2 p.m. this afternoon.
